The affected CIS countries included Russia, Belarus, Uzbekistan, Kyrgyzstan, Tajikistan, and Armenia. The affected targets included government, financial, healthcare and educational institutions. 
The group likely intended to leverage the gained access to exploit it for subsequent hacks or selling it to other threat actors.

name: Unknown threat group targeted Lebanese Beirut-Rafic Al Hariri International Airport in January 2024
description:
An unknown threat group breached the Beirut-Rafic Al Hariri International Airport in Beirut in January 2024, according to Lebanese media.
The attackers compromised the Flight Information Display System (FIDS). They delivered a message on the airport’s screens asserting that Hezbollah and Iran are pushing Lebanon into war against the will of the Lebanese people. Another Lebanese news site claimed the Baggage Handling System (BHS) was disrupted during the intrusion, requiring a switch to manual inspections of luggage.

name: Unknown threat actor targeted website of Libyan central bank with DDoS attacks on 1 April 2024
description:
The Libyan Central Bank (CBL) disclosed in a Facebook statement that an unknown threat actor targeted its online “Foreign Currency Reservation Platform for Individuals” with DDoS attacks on 1 April 2024, affecting the availability of the bank's services. CBL prevented prolonged outages by restricting system access to network addresses registered within Libya.
The Bank additionally reported a DDoS attack against its official website on 3 April 2024.

name: Unknown hackers installed malware on traffic signal system of Gnalp-Steg Tunnel in Liechtenstein on 3 February 2024
description: Unknown hackers installed malware on the server managing the traffic signal system of the Gnalp-Steg Tunnel in Liechtenstein on 3 February 2024, the Liechtenstein Administration announced on 6 February 2024. The malware disrupted the operation of the traffic signal system, requiring a change to manual traffic management and the installation of a temporary traffic light to keep the tunnel open.

name: Emails from Lithuanian Ministry for sale
description: In a data trading forum, 1.6 million emails from the Lithuanian Foreign Ministry were offered for sale. The Lithuanian president also announces that there are indications that sensitive and secret data were stolen in a cyberattack in November 2020.

name: Kaunas University of Technology in Lithuania subjected to network disruptions and data breach on 8 December 2023
description: Kaunas University of Technology in Lithuania reported a breach of its infrastructure that on 8 December 2023 led to disruptions of several systems and the theft of internal information. Exfiltrated data included the name, surname, personal identification number, residential address, telephone number, e-mail address, registration number of private cars of university employees.

name: Lithuanian electric car charging service Ignitis ON disrupted on 11 February 2024
description: Customer-facing infrastructure of the Lithuanian electric car charging service Ignitis ON was disrupted on the evening of 11 February 2024. Ignitis customers were no longer able to access the service's app to charge their car, and all Ignitis charging stations were disconnected from the Internet. The company was able to restore connectivity several hours later. Unnamed threat actors published the data of 20,000 customers allegedly obtained during the intrusion. Extracted information included customer names and addresses. The company started an investigation into the incident and informed the d

name: Luxembourg DDoS 2017
description: DDoS attack takes down Luxembourg government servers

name: Unknown Threat Actors Carried Out a DDoS Attack Against the State Information Technology Centre in Luxembourg on 14 January 2025
description: On Friday, 14 January 2025, a cyberattack was carried out by an unknown entity against the State Information Technology Centre (CTIE) in Luxembourg, affecting the availability of several government websites, including Guichet.lu, MyGuichet.lu, and government.lu, as well as the LuxTrust online banking service. The attack, identified as a DDoS attack, aimed at saturating servers with a large number of queries, occurred during a busy period of activity. It was detected by LuxTrust's protective measures shortly before 2 p.m. and countermeasures were activated. The situation was resolved between 4:

name: Unknown threat actors launched a Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attack on LuxTrust in Luxembourg on 15 January 2025
description:
LuxTrust was targeted by a Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attack on Wednesday, January 15, 2025. The attack made its verification services unavailable for around half an hour, from 11:05, and was confirmed by a LuxTrust spokesperson in an email to the Luxembourg Times on the same day.
This attack followed a similar DDoS attack last Friday, which disrupted several government websites and connected services, including LuxTrust, for two hours.

name: Unknown Threat Actors targeted websites of Macau Public Security Forces with DDoS attacks on 10 July 2024
description:
On 10 July 2024, several websites of Macau's Public Security Forces were targeted. Starting at 8 p.m., DDoS attacks rendered the websites of the Secretariat for Security (GSS), Public Security Police (PSP), Fire Services Bureau (CB), Macau Public Security Forces Affairs Bureau (DSFSM), and Macau Public Security Forces Academy (ESFSM) inaccessible.
Macau, a Special Administrative Region of China, promptly responded under the direction of Secretary for Security Wong Sio Chak. Authorities swiftly collaborated with telecom operators, restoring all affected websites by midnight. The websites of the

name: Unidentified Hackers targeted Malawi Immigration Department's Computer Networks with Ransomware in February 2024
description: Unknown cyber criminals targeted the networks of the Malawi Immigration Department with ransomware in February 2024. The hackers responsible demanded a ransom. President Lazarus Chakwera described the incident as a serious breach of national security and emphasized that Malawi will not negotiate with the threat actors. Due to the incident, Malawi had to suspend the issuance of passports and aimed to identify an interim solution to resume passport issuance within three weeks.

name: Unknown actors spread XorDDoS Trojan to build botnet spanning 21 countries beginning December 2022
description:
Unknown actors infected Linux devices of a variety of organisations across 21 countries with the XorDDoS Trojan between December 2022 and 15 August 2023, IT security company Palo Alto Networks reported on 16 October 2023.
The unidentified threat actors compromised systems of organisations active in the semiconductor, telecommunications and retail industries as well as of transportation and finance companies. Hijacked devices were integrated into a botnet for conducting DDoS attacks.

name: MNDF Website Hacked
description: The Maldives National Defence Force (MNDF) has confirmed that its website was hacked last night by an unknown attacker.

name: RanRan Ransomware deployed against government entitites in the Middle East
description: Malware researchers at Palo Alto Networks discovered a new strain of ransomware, dubbed Ran Ran, that has been used in targeted attacks against government organizations in the Middle East.

name: DDoS-attack on the website of the Mexican National Action Party
description: The website of the Mexican National Action Party is hit by a cyberattack during the final television debate between presidential candidates ahead of the July 1 vote, after the site had published documents critical of the leading candidate.

name: Unknown actors used Pegasus software to spy on four Mexican Public Defender's Office officials in 2021
description:
Unknown hackers spied on four judicial officials working on the controversial Wallace case. 
The Pegasus malware from the Israeli NSO Group was used to infiltrate the victims' mobile phones between May and November 2021, according to the University of Toronto's Citizen Lab. 
According to one of the victims, Salvador Leyva, the former technical secretary of Combating Torture, there seems to be a connection between the infiltration of the six technical devices (mostly phones) and his work as a public defender in the Wallace case, the alleged kidnapping and murder of Hugo Alberto Wallace Miranda

name: Unknown actors gained access into the server of the Mexican Secretariat of Infrastructure, Communications and Transport (SICT) in October 2022
description: The Secretariat of Infrastructure, Communications and Transportation (SICT) made the announcement via Twitter on October 24, that it got hacked. The hack subsequently disrupted the Mexican transportation system because the ministry has stopped issuing new permits, license plates and driver’s licenses for commercial truck operators until Dec. 31, but it did not damage the agency’s systems, nor were citizen’s data compromised.

name: Unknown actors spread XorDDoS Trojan to build botnet spanning 21 countries beginning December 2022
description:
Unknown actors infected Linux devices of a variety of organisations across 21 countries with the XorDDoS Trojan between December 2022 and 15 August 2023, IT security company Palo Alto Networks reported on 16 October 2023.
The unidentified threat actors compromised systems of organisations active in the semiconductor, telecommunications and retail industries as well as of transportation and finance companies. Hijacked devices were integrated into a botnet for conducting DDoS attacks.

name: Unknown actor gained access to Zimbra and Roundcube email servers of private and public institutions targeting government entities of different countries beginning in January 2023
description:
An unknown actor gained access to Zimbra and Roundcube email servers of private and public institutions targeting government entities of different countries beginning in January 2023, the Dutch IT security company EclecticIQ reported on 17 July 2023.
The targeting of government institutions in particular focused on Ukraine, Spain, Indonesia, Brazil, France, and Mexico. Whether the unidentified threat actor managed to break into the email servers of these government organizations was unclear.
EclecticIQ assesses the threat actor exploited vulnerabilities in the Roundcube email servers (CVE-2020

name: Unknown actors targeted Mexican banks with ATM malware "FiXS" in 2023
description: Unknown actors targeted Mexican banks with ATM malware "FiXS" in 2023, acording to IT company Metabase Q. During the attacks, which are directed against ATMs that supports CEN XFS, ATMs are instructed to dispense money 30 minutes after the last ATM reboot. According to Metbase Q, the attackers used the external keyboard for communication and Russian metadata was found in the code.

name: Unknown actors targeted Zimbra-using organizations worldwide with phishing campaign since April 2023
description: Unknown actors have targeted Zimbra-using organizations in a worldwide phishing campaign since April 2023, according to a report by ESET from 17 August 2023. The reported targets were small and medium-sized businesses and government entities, mainly from Poland, Ecuador, and Italy. ESET stated that the targets were connected by their use of Zimbra. Additionally, affected countries included Russia, Kazakhstan, Ukraine, France, Mexico, Brazil, Argentina, Chile, and Peru. According to ESET, it seems likely that the attackers were able to compromise the victims' administrator accounts, allowing th

name: Unknown actors disrupted the municipal administration of Tulancingo, Mexico, using ransomware on 9 May 2023
description: The computer system of three areas of the Municipality of Tulancingo, Mexico, reportedly suffered a ransomware attack designed to extort the local government. The incident is being reported by the Cybernetic Police and there is no confirmation of data theft yet. According an official spokesperson, the compromised systems related to property tax, land registry and transfer of ownership as well as tax execution are down since the attack started on 9 May.

name: Unknown Threat Actors breached the Attorney General's Office of Nuevo León in Mexico in March 2024
description:
In March 2024, a hacker breached the computer system of the Attorney General's Office of Nuevo León, accessing 960 folders and stealing approximately 14.7 GB worth of information containing sensitive data, including photos of corpses and identifications. The attack was detected in March 2024, and an internal investigation was initiated. The Nuevo León Attorney General's Office kept the cyber incident hidden for nine months until the breach was revealed through social media. 
The incident was publicly disclosed on 17 December 2024 by the Attorney General's Office of Nuevo León. They claim to ha

name: Unknown threat actor targeted the Sonora Institute of Technology (ITSON), Mexico, with ransomware on 1 June 2024
description:
An unknown threat actor targeted the Sonora Institute of Technology (ITSON), Mexico, with ransomware on 1 June 2024, disrupting networks and systems.
The university's information technology area was the victim of a ransomware-type computer virus that supports some of the servers that support institutional processes, denying access to information systems and the data contained in them.
To reduce risks to other university systems, wired and wireless connections to the compromised servers were disabled.
This affects the operation of processes, academic and administrative services on all campuses.

name: Unknown threat actor hit website of Mexican Electoral Institute of Mexico City (IECM) on 2 June 2024
description: Unknown threat actor targeted the website of Mexican Electoral Institute of Mexico City (IECM) on 2 June 2024, an advisor of the institute confirmed in an interview. Coinciding with the national elections in Mexico, what appeared to be a DDoS attack affected access to the website and more specifically reporting of the Preliminary Election Results Program (PREP) run by IECM . According to advisor Mauricio Huesca, the IECM website received "a series of attacks" targeting the electoral body's website. The services were restored after a few minutes at around 21:45 hours.

name: Unknown hackers gained unauthorized access to systems of Mexican mining company Fresnillo
description:
Unknown hackers stole data on or around 30 July 2024 from the systems of Fresnillo PLC, a Mexican company involved in the building of mines and the harvesting of raw materials. Fresnillo is the largest supplier of silver and a leading producer of gold, copper, and zinc.
According to the company, the hackers gained unauthorized access to several systems' data, and an investigation was initiated while other response measures were undertaken to secure the company's systems. In a filing with the London Stock Exchange from 30 July, Fresnillo assessed the incident to have no material operational or

name: Mexican mining company Industrias Peñoles experienced network disruptions as of 30 July 2024
description: According to the news outlet El Financiero, the Mexican mining company Industrias Peñoles and its subsidiary Fresnillo experienced network disruptions, starting on 30 July 2024. The company reportedly sent a note to the Mexican Stock Exchange (BMV), outlining that the damage and scope caused by the incident are still being evaluated.

name: Unknown hackers took over the Facebook account of the electoral tribunal of the Mexican state of Tamaulipas on 21 August 2024
description:
Unknown hackers took over the Facebook account of the electoral tribunal of the Mexican state of Tamaulipas on 21 August 2024, the electoral tribunal reported on its website.
In addition, the president of the electoral tribunal is said to have been the victim of an armed attack on the same day.

name: Witchetty targeted governments and a stock market exchange in the Middle East and Africa between February and September 2022
description: The espionage group Witchetty (LookingFrog) targeted governments and a stock exchange in the Middle East and Africa between February and September 2022. They also exploited the ProxyShell and ProxyLogon vulnerabilities and used new tools such as a backdoor Trojan. The goal is a permanent presence in the targets' networks.

name: Unattributed hackers stole data from a Middle Eastern government using DonQuixote malware in February 2024
description:
Unattributed hackers stole data from a Middle Eastern government using DonQuixote malware in February 2024, the Russian IT security company Kaspersky reported on 18 April 2024.
In addition to the Middle Eastern government, other governments around the world, including in Asia-Pacific (APAC), Europe and North America, have also been targeted.

name: Moldova's Court of Accounts
description: Public databases and audits were destroyed in a cyberattack by an unknown attacker on the website of the Moldovan Court of Accounts. The institution shut down the website to ensure an investigation and recovery of the data. The cyberattack coincides with the new Moldova president coming to power, but it's still unclear whether that had anything to do with it.

name: Unknown Threat Actors targeted the website of the Central Election Commission of Moldova with a cyberattack in Moldova on 3 November 2024
description: On 3 November 2024, the day of the second round of the presidential elections in the Republic of Moldova, the servers of the Central Electoral Commission (CEC) were targeted by a cyber attack, according to the statement made on Sunday by Angelica Caraman, the CEC president, in a press conference. These attacks affected the speed of the voting registration application at polling stations, thus hampering the verification and registration process. It was observed a slowing down of the functionality of the program that registers voters at the ballot box resulting most likely from a DDos attack.

name: Unknown threat actors hijacked X account of Myanmar military junta leader Min Aung Hlaing to promote crypto scam on 22 February 2025
description: On 22 February 2025, hackers took control of the official X account of Myanmar's military junta leader Min Aung Hlaing and used it to promote a fake crypto coin, branding it as Myanmar's national crypto currency. The hackers shared and deleted several crypto money wallet addresses, and later claimed the launch had been postponed, raising suspicion among crypto traders.

name: Attack on Dutch Government
description: Unknown hackers hacked several dutch gov websites

name: Ransomware found in the systems of the Tweede Kamer(NLD)
description: Ransomware is found on the computersystems of the Tweede Kamer, the lower house of Dutch parliament.

name: Unknown ransomware group disrupted computer systems of Dutch food wholesaler Heuschen & Schrouff
description: An unknown ransomware group disrupted the computer systems of Dutch food wholesaler Heuschen & Schrouff, the Dutch digital news platform 1Limburg reported based on information from undisclosed sources on 24 August. According to the reports, the incident resulted in the shutdown of company systems. Unnamed hackers issued a ransom demand to restore access.

name: Unnamed threat actor gained access to internal systems of Dutch industrial company Kendrion in August 2023
description:
Kendrion, a Dutch industrial technology company specialising in the design and manufacture of electromagnetic components and systems, recently identified a cybersecurity incident in which an unauthorised third party gained access to their corporate network at an as yet unknown time in August 2023. In response, the company immediately took all of its systems offline as a mitigation measure.
A preliminary investigation did not conclusively rule out that the unknown threat actor accessed company data.
The effects of the intrusion also extended to the German subsidiary of Kendrion in Malente, wher

name: Unknown actor interfered with emergency signal network of health technology provider Tunstall Nederland in mid-November 2023
description: An unknown actor targeted at Tunstall Nederland, the Dutch branch of a Swedish health technology provider specialising in long-term care solutions, on 12 November 2023. The infiltration resulted in a disruption of company systems involved in the relaying of reports from Tunstall's emergency buttons used by ill or injured patients or elderly customers to call for help after a fall. Distress signals sent from buttons did not reach Tunstall's control room, from where they are normally forwarded to emergency rooms. Envida, a welfare organisation, reported that over 3,000 patient buttons across Lim

name: Likely state-backed threat actors hacked a police account and stole data on all Dutch police officers in a data breach in 2024
description: Unknown threat actors compromised a police account and stole data on all Dutch police officers, including names, email addresses, and positions of the entire organisation, capturing also the names, official contact details, e-mail addresses, positions, and ranks of all Dutch police officers, including those working undercover. The perpetrators hacked a police account, which gave access to the main file of the entire police organisation, as disclosed by the Minister of Justice and Security, David van Weel, to the parliament in The Hague on 27 September 2024, with the exact number of people affe

name: Unknown hackers disrupted systems of Dutch pharmaceutical company HAL Allergy on 19 February 2024
description:
Unknown hackers disrupted the systems of the Dutch pharmaceutical company HAL Allergy on 19 February 2024, as part of a ransomware attack. 
According to a press release by HAL, an intrusion led to a disruption of access to parts of HAL's network and data. This interference further caused delays in the processing of orders and delivery of products. HAL warned that some personal data may have been compromised during the incident, including the name, address, date of birth, and HAL Allergy product ordered by customers. To determine the extent of a possible data breach, the company initiated a for

name: Unknown Cybercriminals Encrypt Systems of Dutch communications company AddComm in Ransomware Attack Beginning on 5 May 2024, stealing data from Dutch Bank ABN AMRO and regional tax authority
description: Unknown threat actors launched a ransomware attack on the Dutch customer communications company AddComm, a supplier for ABN AMRO, as disclosed by the Dutch bank on 24 May 2024. The attack occurred between 5 May and 17 May 2024, with the security incident becoming firstly known to AddComm on 17 May 2024, when the systems were encrypted by the cybercriminals. The encryption of AddComm's systems and data, caused their systems to be unavailable to customers in recent days. The attackers potentially accessed data of a limited number of ABN AMRO clients. While no evidence currently suggests misuse o

name: Nexera DeFi Protocol victim of financial theft on 7 August 2024
description:
Nexera, a cryptocurrency blockchain infrastructure protocol, was the victim of a financial theft from an unknown hacker on 7 August 2024.
The attack, which was outlined by a blockchain security firm, saw the theft of roughly $1.2 million in NXRA coins, as well as over $500,000 in USDT stablecoin. The attack was enabled by an exploit within Nexera's proxy contract - a central point of blockchain infrastructure protocols - which allowed the hacker to take control of it and withdraw all NXRA coins. Nexera was forced to halt trading and pause NXRA contracts so that, ideally, the stolen money could

name: Unknown threat actors breached Dutch Eindhoven University of Technology on 11 January 2025
description: Eindhoven University of Technology was targeted by an unknown attacker, who breached the university's computer network on 11 January 2025 at 9 pm, prompting the university to shut down its network and suspend classes until at least 13 January 2025. The exact circumstances are not clear yet and subject to an ongoing internal investigation.

name: Unknown actors hit province of Loyalty Island in New Caledonia with ransomware on 8 December 2023
description:
The provincial administration of Loyalty Island (La province des îles Loyauté) in New Caledonia, France, was targeted in a ransomware attack on 8 December 2023. 
Access to work stations and operational data was blocked. The first files were encrypted at 3 am, according to the provincial director of digital and communications information system. An undisclosed group demanded one million US dollars as a ransom.

name: Unknown threat actor target Internet Service Provider in French Territory of New Caledonia with DDoS Attacks on 21 May 2024
description:
An unknown threat actor targeted Internet service provider in French Territory of New Caledonia with DDoS Attacks on 21 May 2024, local authorities disclosed in a press conference on 22 May 2024. According to the officials, the incident lasted for several hours and caused short disruptions of the Internet access across the archipelago.
However, some statements raised a number of questions on the target. While one official only named the Internet service provider, others referred to "a huge number of organizations," including "banks."
Christophe Gygès, a member of the local government in charge

name: Unknown hackers target New Caledonian Internet Service Provider Lagoon with Ransomware Attack on 18 August 2024
description:
Unknown hackers targeted the New Caledonian Internet service provider Lagoon with Ransomware. On 19 August, the company announced on social media that it had been the target of ransomware the day before.
The cyberattack paralysed Lagoon's computer systems, and the hackers demanded a ransom in exchange for not leaking exfiltrated data.
Lagoon's website was unavailable. In addition, several subscribers have reported difficulties, including the complete loss of their Internet connection.

name: Unknown actors compromised networks across various industry sectors and several countries using Trigona ransomware beginning in December 2022
description:
Unknown actors compromised the networks of at least 15 targets in various industry sectors across several countries using the relatively unknown Trigona ransomware beginning in December 2022, according to a technical reporty by Palo Alto Networks. 
The 15 targets are organisations in the manufacturing, finance, construction, agriculture, marketing, and high-tech industries with a presence in the US, Italy, France, Germany, Australia, and New Zealand.

name: Unknown actors gained access to vulnerable routers used by a municipal government and medium-sized businesses using HiatusRAT since at least June 2022
description:
Unknown actors gained access to vulnerable routers used by a municipal government and medium-sized businesses using HiatusRAT since at least June 2022, according to technical reports by Black Lotus Labs. 
The affected businesses are consulting firms, IT service providers, and pharmaceutical companies from Latin America, Europe, and North America. Out of more than 4,100 exposed connected routers, at least 100 showed signs of compromise with the possibility of data exfiltration. 
The vulnerable routers are DrayTek Vigor models 2960 and 3900.
In addition to the opportunistic collection of data, t

name: Unknown threat actor targets government entities in the Asia Pacific and North America regions with PureCrypter downloader
description: An unknown threat actor targeted government entities in the Asia Pacific and North America regions with the PureCrypter downloader distributed via Discord since February 2023, according to Menlo Security. The actors also delivered several types of malware including Redline Stealer, AgentTesla, Eternity, Blackmoon and Philadelphia ransomware during the campaign. Apart from the successful compromise of at least some targets, Menlo Security disclosed no specific information about impact.

name: Phishing Norways National Security Authority
description: Norway's National Security Authority (NSM) on Friday confirmed that systems associated with the country's energy and defence sectors were hit with a cyber attack, resulting in a loss of sensitive information.

name: Attack on Norway after Lybia Bombing
description: The Norwegian military has been the victim of a serious cyber attack , a day after Norwegian F-16 fighter jets for the first time carried out bombings in Libya. According to military officials, no sensitive information was lost.

name: Microsoft Exchange Hack: European Banking Authority (EBA) and Norwegian Parliament
description: Starting in January 2021, a significant data breach involving Microsoft Exchange servers occurred. The groups involved in the data breach include Hafnium, which Microsoft alleges to be a Chinese state-sponsored group, along with Tick (also known as Bronze Butler), LuckyMouse (also known as APT27 and Emissary Panda), Calypso, the Winnti Group (also known as BARIUM and APT41), Tonto Team (also known as CactusPete), Mikroceen (also known as Vicious Panda), Websiic, DLTMiner, and at least one previously unidentified group. Apart from DLTMiner, which has been linked to crypto-mining, all the other

name: Unknown actors used JavaScript-code BrownFlood to target mainly Ukrainian websites with DDoS since March 2022
description: Ukraine's computer emergency response team (CERT-UA), in cooperation with the National Bank of Ukraine (CSIRT-NBU), identified and warned about DDoS attacks that targeted pro-Ukrainian sites, including a government web portal, and were disseminating from compromised WordPress sites using the malicious JavaScript-code BrownFlood (CERT-UA#4553). Hackers inserted the malicious script into WordPress sites so that visitors' browsers unwittingly performed Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks on the following websites: https://stop-russian-desinformation.near.page; https://gfsis.org/ (inacces

name: Unknown actors spread XorDDoS Trojan to build botnet spanning 21 countries beginning December 2022
description:
Unknown actors infected Linux devices of a variety of organisations across 21 countries with the XorDDoS Trojan between December 2022 and 15 August 2023, IT security company Palo Alto Networks reported on 16 October 2023.
The unidentified threat actors compromised systems of organisations active in the semiconductor, telecommunications and retail industries as well as of transportation and finance companies. Hijacked devices were integrated into a botnet for conducting DDoS attacks.

name: Unknown threat actor exploited zero-day vulnerability in Ivanti Endpoint Manager Mobile to compromise ICT platform used by Norwegian ministries in July 2023
description:
On 24 July, the Norwegian government revealed a cyberattack affecting against an ICT platform maintained by the Norwegian Government Security and Service Organisation (DSS). The platform is used by all ministries, except the Office of the Prime Minister, the Ministry of Defence, the Ministry of Justice and Public Security and the Ministry of Foreign Affairs.
In an update one day after an initial press briefing from 24 July 2023, the Norwegian National Security Authority (NSM) and the Norwegian Government Security and Service Organisation (DSS) disclosed that the threat actor developed access t

name: Unknown actors deployed multi-stage RAT Snip3 Crypter against commercial and critical infrastructure targets beginning in January 2022
description:
Unknown actors deployed the multi-stage remote access trojan (RAT) Snip3 Crypter against commercial and critical infrastructure targets beginning in January 2022, according to a technical report by Zscaler. 
The sectors affected are healthcare; energy, oil and gas; manufacturing; materials; retail; technology; and finance.
The malware in question is marketed under a crypter-as-a-service model, potentially giving multiple actors access to the same core set of tools. The technical report outlines multiple campaigns without further specifying the organizations targeted during individual campaigns

name: Multiple threat actors infected mobile financial apps using the Android banking malware Nexus since June 2022
description:
Multiple threat actors infected mobile financial apps using the Android banking malware Nexus since June 2022, Italian IT security firm Cleafy reported on 21 March 2023. 
Following the promotion of the Nexus malware in several underground hacker forums in early January 2023, offering Nexus as a malware-as-a-service (MaaS) package for $3,000 per month, several unidentified threat actors are believed to have picked up the tool.
Nexus contains a list of 450 pre-set targets as well as the possibility to request custom targeting capabilities. These configurations have made it difficult to pinpoint

name: Multiple threat actors accessed the networks of the Foreign Affairs Office of an ASEAN member and exported data
description: Multiple threat actors accessed the networks of the Foreign Affairs Office of an ASEAN member and exported data from the mailboxes of targeted officials. According to a report by Elastic Security Labs, the actors used a backdoor Elastic named "SiestaGraph" in the still ongoing operation.

name: Info stealer "SYS01 stealer" targeted critical government infrastructure employees via Facebook Business Accounts since November 2022
description: According to IT vendor Morphisec, the info stealer dubbed "SYS01 stealer" targeted critical government infrastructure employees via their Facebook Business Accounts since November 2022. This campaign was first detected in May 2022 but initially attributed to the Ducktailer Operation by ZScaler. The attackers successfully evaded discovery over a period of five months (November 2022 - March 2023).

name: Unknown actors stole millions from cryptocurrency platform Exactly Protocol
description: Unknown actors stole millions of dollars in cryptocurrency from crypto decentralised finance platform Exactly Protocol. While initial estimates placed the losses for Exactly Protocol at $12 million, a later statement stated the value at $7.3 million in cryptocurrency lost in the course of the attack, which forced the financial platform to halt most services, only allowing customers to withdraw assets while an investigation proceeded.

name: Unknown actors skimmed payment details off websites of food and retail companies
description: Unknown actors compromised the websites of food and retail companies, Akamai Technologies reported in a technical report published on 9 October 2023. Using novel obfuscation techniques, the threat actors leveraged the Magecart malware framework to intercept potentially sensitive payment details of customers using the targeted e-commerce sites.

name: Unknown actors gained access to the Northern Irish data management company Evide and stole information from their customers
description:
Unknown actors gained access to the Northern Irish data management company Evide and stole information from their customers, Irish online newspaper The Journal reported on 17 April 2023.
The Minister of State at the Department of Public Expenditure and Reform and at the Department of the Environment, Climate and Communications Ossian Smyth revealed that Evide's affected customers included a number of community and voluntary organizations, as well as four organizations that care for people who have experienced sexual abuse or rape. These included the charity One in Four, which serves adult surv
receiver_subcategory: ['Civil service / administration', 'Advocacy / activists (e.g. human rights organizations)']

name: Unknown threat actors exploited vulnerabilities within smart contract language Vyper and stole crypto-assets from multiple crypto-platforms in July 2023
description: Unknown threat actors exploited unknown vulnerabilities within the smart contract language Vyper and stole crypto-assets from multiple crypto-platforms in July 2023. The programming team behind Vyper revealed on 30 July that the latest versions of its compiler did fail to correctly implement safeguards against so-called "reentrancy attacks". As a result, at least five cryptocurrency platforms lost more than $25 million worth of crypto-assets so far. Among the victims are the following entities: AlchemixFi ($13 million), JPEG'd ($11 million), MetronomeDAO ($1.6 million), Ellipsis Finance ($68,6

name: Unknown actors use zero-day vulnerability to compromise NetScaler ADC and gateway applications of government organisations beginning late August 2023
description:
Unknown actors have been using a zero-day vulnerability to compromise NetScaler ADC and gateway applications of government organisations, among others, beginning late August 2023, US IT security firm Mandiant reported on 17 October 2023.
This vulnerability (CVE-2023-4966) allowed hackers to steal session data or take over active sessions. Session data obtained in this vein may facilitate threat actors to regain access even after deployment of the patch closing the reported vulnerability. In addition to government organisations, Mandiant also observed session hijacking at professional services

name: An active attack campaign exploits vulnerabilities in Ray, a widely used open-source AI framework, since at least 5 September 2023, affecting companies and institutions in various sectors
description:
As discovered by the research team of Israeli cyber security firm Oligo, unknown hackers actively exploit vulnerabilities in open-source AI framework Ray since at least 5 September 2023. Accordingly, it is the first known attack campaign targeting AI Workloads actively exploited.
Thousands of companies using the AI infrastructure are currently exposed to the campaign, hundreds have been compromised. Several sectors are already affected by active exploitation such as those of education, cryptocurrency, biopharma, medicine, video analytics and cloud services
CVE-2023-48022 is one of the vulnerab

name: Unknown hackers disrupted touristic and cultural websites of the city of Rome on 11 September 2023
description:
Unknown hackers disrupted various websites of the city of Rome on 11 September 2023, event planner and website operator Zetema reported. 
Affected websites included those of organisations that had contracted Zetema for web management, as well as the online portals of museums in the municipality of Rome, the supervisory authority of the Ministry of Cultural Assets and Activities in Rome, the museum pass MIC and other several other touristic services and cultural offerings, such as the La Casina di Raffaello children's leisure centre and the Arte Bellezza Cultura (ABC) cultural project. Many of

name: Hong Kong-based crypto business Mixin hit by $200 million theft
description: Mixin Network, a Hong Kong-based cryptocurrency exchange, was the victim of a $200 million theft from unknown actors on 23 September 2023, making it the largest crypto theft of 2023 at the time of reporting and the 10th largest of all time, according to Reuters. The attack was leveraged through an unnamed cloud service provider for Mixin Network, which allowed the hackers to access the funds. As a result of the breach, Mixin temporarily suspended deposit and withdrawal services while it patched the vulnerabilities that led to the attack.

name: Unknown hackers exploited a vulnerability in the smart contract of the cryptocurrency BOGE and stole an unspecified amount of it
description:
Unknown hackers exploited a vulnerability in the smart contract of the cryptocurrency BOGE and stole an unspecified amount of it, the associated company itself reported on X on 27 May 2024.
According to the report, the unknown hacker used a flash loan to mint additional tokens fraudulently.

name: Unknown hacker exploited vulnerability in cryptocurrency platform Abracadabra and stole assets worth $6.5 million on 30 January 2024
description: An unknown hacker exploited a vulnerability in certain older Cauldron V4 codes of the cryptocurrency platform Abracadabra and stole $6.5 million worth of the associated stablecoin Magic Internet Money (MIM) on 30 January 2024, Abracadabra confirmed via social media on the day of the incident.

name: Hacker Exploited Seneca Stablecoin Protocol and Stole $6.4 Million Worth of Crypto Assets on 28 February 2024
description: On 28 February 2024, Seneca, a decentralised finance (DeFi) platform, suffered an exploitation of a vulnerability in its stablecoin protocol. The unidentified hacker manipulated the Seneca smart contract and siphoned off $6.4 million worth of crypto assets, affecting both Ethereum and Arbitrum networks. CertiK and Blocksec experts identified the cause of the breach as an arbitrary call issue exacerbated by the fact that Seneca did not have a pause function integrated into their contracts. Seneca was able to recover 80% of the funds from the threat actor in exchange for a pay-out of the 20% of

name: Unknown threat actor exploited old Dolomite Exchange contract And Stole $1.8 Million Worth Of Crypto Assets On 20 March 2024
description:
An unknown threat actor targeted the Dolomite Exchange protocol on 20 March 2024, the company confirmed on social media. According to a report from blockchain security platform CertiK, an old Dolomite Exchange contract from 2019 for Ethereum had been targeted through an approval exploit, resulting in a loss of approximately $1.8 million worth of cryptocurrency. 
Following the incident, Dolomite announced a settlement with the threat actor, who returned 90% of the stolen funds in exchange for retaining the remaining 10%.

name: Security research activity gained access to meeting information and associated metadata using vulnerability in Cisco's WebEx
description:
Security research activity gained access to meeting information and associated metadata using a vulnerability in Cisco's virtual conferencing solution WebEx, the company disclosed on 4 June 2024.
This affected both the Cisco WebEx programmes on customers' local computers and the cloud-based solution. Cisco identified the vulnerability at the beginning of May and fixed it by 28 May 2024, after which Cisco did not detect any further unauthorised access. Cisco initially reported that the unauthorized access was only observed in connection to a data centre site in Frankfurt. Subsequent investigati

name: Unknown hackers drained $20 million worth of cryptocurrencies by attacking Sonne Finance’s USD Coin and Wrapped Ether (WETH) contracts on 14 May 2024
description: Unknown hackers attacked Sonne Finance’s USD Coin and Wrapped Ether (WETH) contracts on 14 May 2024. Due to this attack, the attackers stole $20 million in WETH, Velo (VELO), soVELO and Wrapped USDC (USDC.e). Accordingly, Sonne Finance announced on 15 May that all markets on Optimism have been paused. Soon after, the protocol partnered with Cyvers to investigate the situation further. Sonne is currently exploring all options to retrieve the stolen funds, including negotiating a bug bounty for the hacker.

name: Unknown actor gained access to the systems of the BBC Pension Scheme on 21 May 2024
description: The British Broadcasting Corporation (BBC) informed the members of its pension scheme about the data breach on 29 May 2024. The breach occurred on 21 May 2024 and resulted in the data of over 25.000 members being stolen. The stolen data includes members' names, date of birth, sex, home address and national insurance number. The BBC Pension Scheme with the BBC Pension Trust is one of the largest occupational pension schemes in the UK.

name: Unknown hackers gained access to decentralized crypto exchange Velocore on 2 June 2024
description:
Unknown hackers gained access to the decentralized exchange Velocore on 2 June 2024. The hack cost users approximately $7-10 million in lost liquidity.
The hackers managed to transfer some of the stolen currency to the Ethereum mainnet. Velocore operates on the Linea blockchain and was forced to halt block production due to the hack. To breach the Velocore's systems, the hackers exploited a vulnerability within the Balancer-style CPMM pool contract.

name: An unknown threat actor targeted DeFi lending protocol UwU Lend on 10 June 2024
description:
An unknown threat actor targeted DeFi lending protocol UwU Lend on 10 June 2024, according to multiple reportings by blockchain security companies. The compromise led to a loss of digital assets of approximately $19.3M.
According to them, the theft targeting UwU Lend lasted six minutes and the attackers conducted three transactions within this time. Following the warnings of other blockchain companies, UwU Lend informed their customers that the protocol was paused and the situation investigated. According to media reporting, the stolen funds include major cryptocurrencies such as WBTC and stab

name: Unknown attackers redirected web traffic of crypto platforms Compound Finance and Pendle to phishing copies starting on 11 July 2024
description:
Unknown attackers hijacked the domains of crypto platform Compound Finance and DeFi protocol Pendle through the domain hosting service of US SaaS company SquareSpace since 11 July 2024.
Users accessing the websites of Compound Labs and Pendle were redirected to phishing copies of the websites to extract their account information. 
Both sites issued warnings via their social media pages to stop visiting their sites until further notice. They advised users to change their passwords immediately if they had entered them on the websites.
Apart from the domains, none of the internal systems were aff

name: Unknown Attackers Gained Unauthorized Access To Cryptocurrency platform MonoSwap To Steal its funds On 23 July 2024
description:
Unknown attackers gained unauthorized access to cryptocurrency platform MonoSwap to steal funds on 23 July 2024. According to the platform, one of its developers accidentally installed a malicious phishing app on 23 July 2024 and was lured into a call with scammers posing as venture capitalists. At some point during the call, the scammers installed malware on the developer's computer, which reportedly had access to all the platform's wallets and contracts. This allowed the hackers to withdraw the majority of the staked funds on the platform.
The platform experienced a significant withdrawal of

name: Unidentified actors exploited vulnerability to steal $5.28-$6.8 million from Terra Blockchain
description: Terra Blockchain fell victim to a theft of between $5.28 and $6.8 million carried out by unknown actors on 31 July 2024. In response, the platform stopped block production and transactions while an emergency patch was implemented to fix the exploited vulnerability that enabled the theft. Based on assessment by the smart contract auditing service Besoin and Terra, the hackers exploited a reentrance vulnerability in IBC hooks, a third-party module that facilitating the movement of tokens. The vulnerability had been patched within other blockchains, but an update put out by Terra left out this pa

name: Unknown threat actors attacked Telegram trading bot Banana Gun and stole cryptocurrency about 1.4 million dollars worth in September 2024
description: Unknown threat actors attacked the Telegram trading bot Banana Gun and stole cryptocurrency which is about 1.4 million dollars worth in September 2024. Due to this attack, the wallets of about 36 users got drained. According to Cyvers, the attack does not point to a wider smart contract vulnerability. The number of victims suggests that the hacker didn’t successfully infiltrate the entire trading bot, but an isolated number of accounts. According to Banana Gun itself, the back-end is not compromised, and they believe the issue may stem from a front-end vulnerability. The bot got switched off i

name: Unknown Hackers Breached Spanish Insurer Mutua Madrileña's Home Insurance Customer Database on 23 September 2024
description: On September 23, 2024, Mutua Madrileña suffered a cyberattack targeting its home insurance customer database through one of its external providers. Although the insurer swiftly rectified the incident, the data of thousands of clients may have been compromised. In response to the breach, Mutua Madrileña acted promptly and transparently, informing affected customers via email and reporting the incident to the Spanish Data Protection Agency.

name: Unknown threat actors promoted fake airdrop campaign via hijacked social media account of EigenLayer on 18 October 2024
description: On October 18, 2024, the official X account of EigenLayer, a restructuring protocol, was hijacked by unknown threat actors, who launched a fake airdrop campaign, directing users to request EIGEN tokens through malicious links. Sector experts, including Blockchain detective ZachXBT and Polygon Labs CISO Mudit Gupta, quickly warned users about the scam through Telegram and other channels. The attackers shared multiple posts, including a 'last call' message, attempting to fool users.

name: Unknown Threat Actors Hacked the Ministry of Education and Science (MEC) Data Center of Paraguay on 21 October 2024
description: Unknown threat actors hacked the Ministry of Education and Science (MEC) data center of Paraguay on 21 October 2024, compromising all its data center, with services and systems dropped, and demanded 16 Bitcoins (approximately 1,076,910 dollars) in exchange for restoring the system. However, the MEC denied the ransom request. According to officials, all information held in the data centre, including data of administrative officials, teachers, the Single Student Registry (RUE) and the entire Educational Information and Management System of the MEC, is at risk due to the lack of backup copies, th

name: Unknown Hacker Stole $2.8M USD From SUNRAY Cryptocurrency Platform Between 30-31 October 2024
description: According to CertiK, an unknown hacker gained access to private keys within the network of SUNRAY, a cryptocurrency platform, at the end of October 2024. Access to the private keys enabled the hacker to steal almost $3 million USD from SUNRAY, which were then transferred to other crypto accounts. According to SUNRAY, an investigation was initiated in order to solve the problem of compromised data within the theft.

name: An Unnamed Hacker Exploited A Vulnerability And Withdrew A Total Of $25.5 Million Worth Of Tokens From The Thala DeFi Platform On 15 November 2024
description:
An undisclosed hacker exploited a vulnerability in the Thala DeFi platform on 15 November 2024 and withdrew tokens with a total value of 25.5 million dollars. Thala, a decentralized finance protocol on the Aptos blockchain, was quickly able to identify the attacker, negotiate a bounty and recover the assets that had been frozen in the meantime. 
The protocol's codebase is currently undergoing a comprehensive review and re-audit of all affected and related packages, and Thala said in a statement that it will return all stolen funds to user accounts once it has re-audited its codebase.

name: Unknown Threat Actors Breached And Stole Sensitive Patient Information From Everyones Medical House In Denmark Between 1 December 2023 And 30 December 2023
description: At the beginning of December 2024, the controversial medical group Everyones Medical House was subjected to an extensive cyber attack by an unknown group of hackers, who exploited the access to steal person-sensitive information about patients. The attack resulted in patients being unable to log in or get in touch with their doctors' office. The clinic handled the attack with emergency preparedness to maintain acute functions and reported the case to the Data Protection Agency and the police on 30 December 2024. The incident was publicly disclosed by Everyones Doctors Office on their website o

name: Unknown advanced threat actor targeted multiple government and government-related targets using a FortiOS vulnerability
description: Fortinet reports that a sudden system stop and subsequent startup failure of several FortiGate units belonging to a customer prompted an investigation of attacks aimed at a vulnerability in FortiOS (FG-IR-22-369 / CVE-2022-41328). Due to the complexity of the attack, the company is suspecting an advanced threat actor, without disclosing any further details. Exploitations of the vulnerability focus on governments and large organizations.

name: American Express Card Member data exposed through data breach at third-party merchant processor in the beginning of March 2024
description: As disclosed in a data breach notice to their customers by American Express, a global provider of financial services based in the US, a merchant processor tasked with handling American Express Card member data was compromised in the beginning of March 2024. Information of American Express card members, including names, account numbers and expiration dates, may have been exposed.

name: Unknown threat actor targets government entities in the Asia Pacific and North America regions with PureCrypter downloader
description: An unknown threat actor targeted government entities in the Asia Pacific and North America regions with the PureCrypter downloader distributed via Discord since February 2023, according to Menlo Security. The actors also delivered several types of malware including Redline Stealer, AgentTesla, Eternity, Blackmoon and Philadelphia ransomware during the campaign. Apart from the successful compromise of at least some targets, Menlo Security disclosed no specific information about impact.

name: Operation Pawn Storm 2007
description: Fancy Bear attacked the military and defense contractors of the US and some of their allies in a longterm espionage campaign, with the usage of some Zerodays

name: Bitten by Rats
description: Pakistan Government Officials Targeted with RATs in Cyber-Espionage Campaign

name: Unnamed hacker group targeted telecom and government sectors in Asia since 2021
description: Unnamed hacker group was targeting Telecommunications and Government organizations in Kazakhstan, Uzbekistan, Pakistan, and Vietnam since 2021. Security firm Check Point tracked related tools under the campaign 'Staying Alive'. While not sharing any clear overlaps with known actors, the tools showed connections to a set of infrastructure previously tied by Kaspersky to an actor tracked as ToddyCat. Kaspersky identified ToddyCat as China-affiliated with an activity profile that matches the operating region Check Point observed for Staying Alive.

name: Pakistan FBR
description: Unknown hackers attacked the Federal Board of Revenue (FBR) and disrupted websites on Pakistan's Independence Day (August 14). In addition, the hackers sold the FBR's network access for $26,000 via a Russian cybercrime forum.

name: Unidentified actor gained access to a Pakistani government entity, a public sector bank and a telecommunications provider using the ShadowPad backdoor beginning in mid-February 2022
description:
An unidentified actor gained access to a Pakistani government entity, a public sector bank, and a telecommunications provider using the ShadowPad backdoor beginning in mid-February 2022, the Japanese IT security firm Trend Micro reported in a technical report on 14 July 2023.
Trend Micro assessed the unattributed actors had tempered with the "E-Office" application by planting the ShadowPad backdoor in a compromised installer. E-Office is a custom application in use by Pakistani government entities only and designed to help deliver administrative services in a paperless and efficient manner. On

name: Unknown actors spread XorDDoS Trojan to build botnet spanning 21 countries beginning December 2022
description:
Unknown actors infected Linux devices of a variety of organisations across 21 countries with the XorDDoS Trojan between December 2022 and 15 August 2023, IT security company Palo Alto Networks reported on 16 October 2023.
The unidentified threat actors compromised systems of organisations active in the semiconductor, telecommunications and retail industries as well as of transportation and finance companies. Hijacked devices were integrated into a botnet for conducting DDoS attacks.

name: Unidentified threat actor targeted Pakistani government organisation exploiting Zimbra vulnerability in August 2023
description:
An unidentified threat actor targeted a Pakistani government organization by exploiting a vulnerability in a web client managing access to Zimbra email servers (CVE-2023-37580) in August 2023, according to Google's Threat Analysis Group (TAG). By using the exploit, the hackers were able to steal the Zimbra authentication token, which was exfiltrated to ntcpk[.]org.
In the same report, TAG disclosed three other campaigns exploiting the same vulnerability, spanning from 29 July 2023 until 25 August 2023,two of which predated the public disclosure of the vulnerability (13 July) and patch release

name: Unknown hackers defaced Pakistani Ministry of Aviation's website on 21 November 2023
description: Unknown hackers defaced the Pakistani Ministry of Aviation's website on 21 November 2023 and posted several messages in a foreign language on the website. The website was later shut down and restored soon after.

name: Unknown actors exfiltrate corporate data of Pak Suzuki Motor Company Limited (PSMCL), Pakistan, on 9 April 2024
description: On 9 April 2024 Pak Suzuki Motor Company Limited (PSMCL), Pakistan, discovered that corporate data had been stolen. They published their finding in a stock filing on 15 April 2024.

name: Unknown Threat Actor Targets Various Sectors in Hong Kong and Pakistan in Espionage Campaign "Operation Cobalt Whisper" Since May 2024
description: According to the Indian software company Seqrite, an unspecified threat actor targets various sectors in espionage campaign "Operation Cobalt Whisper" since May 2024. The new campaign targets the defence and research sectors in South Asia, particularly Pakistan and Hong Kong, but with growing interest in India. The focus is on engineering researchers and professors, using sophisticated lures like decoy documents related to electrotechnical societies and energy infrastructure. The campaign employs the post-exploitation tool Cobalt Strike, indicating a methodical approach to cyber-espionage. Seq

name: Unknown Threat Actors Crashed Dewan Farooque Motors Limited (DFML) IT Servers In Pakistan Using Malware In A Cyberattack On 28 November 2024
description: On 28 November 2024, unknown threat actors carried out a malware cyberattack on Dewan Farooque Motors Limited (DFML), crashing the company's IT servers and corrupting its data. The attack led to the postponement of the DFML board of directors meeting, which was originally scheduled for 28 November 2024. The incident was disclosed by DFML in a stock filing on 30 November 2024, and reported by Dawn, a Pakistani English-language newspaper, on the same day. The company stated that the restoration of its information system and financial data, including that of the first quarter ending 30 September

name: Unknown Perpetrator Breached the Papua New Guinea Internal Revenue Commission in a Ransomware Attack on 28 January 2025
description: The Papua New Guinea tax office, specifically the Internal Revenue Commission (IRC), was hit by a ransomware attack on 28 January 2025, resulting in a system outage that affected its Standard Integrated Tax Accounting System (SIGTAS), internet networks, phones, and email communications. The attack may have exposed sensitive data belonging to Australian companies and individuals. The IRC engaged a private company to assist in its recovery. Australia offered cybersecurity experts to support the PNG government. The incident was reported by ABC News on 13 February 2025. The attack remained under i

name: Unknown actors spread XorDDoS Trojan to build botnet spanning 21 countries beginning December 2022
description:
Unknown actors infected Linux devices of a variety of organisations across 21 countries with the XorDDoS Trojan between December 2022 and 15 August 2023, IT security company Palo Alto Networks reported on 16 October 2023.
The unidentified threat actors compromised systems of organisations active in the semiconductor, telecommunications and retail industries as well as of transportation and finance companies. Hijacked devices were integrated into a botnet for conducting DDoS attacks.

name: Unknown actors targeted Zimbra-using organizations worldwide with phishing campaign since April 2023
description: Unknown actors have targeted Zimbra-using organizations in a worldwide phishing campaign since April 2023, according to a report by ESET from 17 August 2023. The reported targets were small and medium-sized businesses and government entities, mainly from Poland, Ecuador, and Italy. ESET stated that the targets were connected by their use of Zimbra. Additionally, affected countries included Russia, Kazakhstan, Ukraine, France, Mexico, Brazil, Argentina, Chile, and Peru. According to ESET, it seems likely that the attackers were able to compromise the victims' administrator accounts, allowing th

name: Services of the Geological Mining and Metallurgical Institute of Peru (INGEMMET) were disrupted by cybercriminals in July 2024
description:
Services of the Geological Mining and Metallurgical Institute of Peru (INGEMMET) were disrupted by cybercriminals in July 2024, according to the Institute on 17 July 2024. As a result, for several days (at the time of reporting), no new applications for mining claims have been received.
Accordingly, Ingemmet issued a statement warning that its services and applications are temporarily out of service.

name: An Unidentified Threat Actor, Using Alias 'kzoldyck,' Leaked 3.7 TB of Sensitive Data Of 3 Million People From Peruvian Financial Services Provider Interbank On 30 October 2024
description: An unidentified threat actor, using the alias 'kzoldyck', breached the systems of Peruvian financial services provider Interbank, formally the Banco Internacional del Perú Service Holding S.A.A., and leaked 3.7 TB of stolen information online, including personal info, credit card details, CVVs, passwords, and API credentials, related to 3 million customers, after a two-week ransom negotiation failed, with Interbank confirming the data breach and disclosing the incident on October 31, 2024, and assuring customers that their funds were not impacted by the security incident, while the company res

name: SignSight
description: Private companies and government agencies in Vietnam and Philippines attacked during "Operation SignSight".

name: Agent Tesla threat actor "Fucosreal" used the Agent Tesla stealer in data exfiltration campaign against several industries from China, India, the UAE, and the Philippines in March 2024
description: According to a report by CheckPoint Research, Fucosreal, an Agent Tesla threat actor, used the Agent tesla stealer in a spam campaign for the purpose of data exfiltration against several industries from China, India, the UAE, and the Philippines in March 2024. Among the affected sectors are the Diamond industry, Metallurgical industry, Glass manufacturing, Environmentally friendly packaging, Ocean freight shipping, Wallpaper manufacturing and Aluminum industry. The threat actor distributed a malicious TAR archive, which contained the Agent Tesla stealer in the spam campaign and used the Telegr

name: Unknown threat actors targeted website of Philippines House of Representatives with DDoS attacks on 13 March 2024
description: Unknown threat actors targeted the website of the Philippines' House of Representatives with DDoS attacks on 13 March 2024, the Office of the Secretary-General of the House of Representatives confirmed in a press statement. The statement noted that the attack peaked shortly before 3pm PST.

name: Unknown actors compromised the Philippine Bureau of Customs (BoC) on 7 April 2024
description:
Unknown actors compromised the Philippine Bureau of Customs (BoC) on 7 April 2024, the BoC reported on 9 April 2024.
Specifically, the hackers gained access to the BoC's Help Desk Ticketing System and Management Information Dashboard. The hackers compromised accounts and server as well as external cloud-based online applications using compromised login credentials.
There is also the possibility that data was stolen, including names, email addresses, company names, contact details, tax identification numbers and even information on the movement of goods and trade secrets.

name: Unknown Threat Actors Stole Credentials of Employees of 30 Companies Across 12 Industries Worldwide Beginning in Early July 2024
description:
Beginning in at least July 2024, unknown threat actors launched a sophisticated global phishing campaign targeting employees of 30 companies across 12 industries, distributing over 200 malicious links designed to steal user login credentials, with stolen credentials sent to attackers in real time via C2 servers or Telegram bots. Cybersecurity researchers at Group-IB reported on 11 December 2024. 
The industries targeted in this phishing operation included Energy, Fashion, Finance, Aerospace, Manufacturing, Telecommunications, Government, IT, Healthcare, Technology, and Entertainment. 
This cam

name: Website of Philippine Energy Department was targeted on 27 July 2024
description: The website of the Government Energy Management Project (GEMP), part of the Philippines' Department of Energy, was targeted by yet unidentified actors on 27 July 2024. A statement by the Department published the following day said that the website was "hacked and defaced" before it was taken down by the authority. The Philippines' National Computer Emergency Response Team are conducting an investigation before the website will turn online again.

name: Operation Pawn Storm 2007
description: Fancy Bear attacked the military and defense contractors of the US and some of their allies in a longterm espionage campaign, with the usage of some Zerodays

name: Unknown actors targeted Zimbra-using organizations worldwide with phishing campaign since April 2023
description: Unknown actors have targeted Zimbra-using organizations in a worldwide phishing campaign since April 2023, according to a report by ESET from 17 August 2023. The reported targets were small and medium-sized businesses and government entities, mainly from Poland, Ecuador, and Italy. ESET stated that the targets were connected by their use of Zimbra. Additionally, affected countries included Russia, Kazakhstan, Ukraine, France, Mexico, Brazil, Argentina, Chile, and Peru. According to ESET, it seems likely that the attackers were able to compromise the victims' administrator accounts, allowing th

name: Unknown threat actors targeted Zambrów Co-operative Bank in Poland with ransomware on 16 January 2024
description: Unknown threat actors targeted the Zambrów Co-operative Bank in Poland with ransomware. On 16 January 2024, disruptions of the bank's online financial services led to the discovery that customer data had been encrypted. The service disruptions temporarily prevented customers from accessing funds and making payments. While the bank did not specify whether data had been exfiltration during the breach, it cancelled and reissued bank cards for some its customers.

name: Cyberattack on Portuguese military resulting in theft and doxing of NATO documents in 2022
description: The Armed Forces General Staff agency of Portugal (EMGFA) allegedly suffered a prolonged and undetected cyberattack which resulted in NATO documents being stolen and sold on the dark web. The documents were discovered by American cyber-intelligence agents and notified US authorities at the US Embassy in Lisbon. The National Security Office (GNS) and Portugal’s national cybersecurity center immediately reacted to the threat by deploying a team of experts to EMGFA to investigate the defence agency's network. The stolen documents were acquired through specially programmed bots that probed the net

name: Unknown Threat Actor Disrupted operations of Health Service in Madeira in August 2023
description: The Regional Health Service of the Autonomous Region of Madeira (SESARAM) fell victim to a cyberattack aimed at disrupting its operations. The attack led to the suspension of all non-urgent clinical activities scheduled for 7 August 2023, affecting consultations, surgeries, clinical analyses and complementary means used for diagnosis. SESARAM has reported the incident to the relevant authorities, including the National Cyber Security Centre and the National Data Protection Commission. As of 8 August, there was no evidence that clinical records had been compromised. SESARAM has refrained from l

name: Unknown Threat Actors Stole Credentials of Employees of 30 Companies Across 12 Industries Worldwide Beginning in Early July 2024
description:
Beginning in at least July 2024, unknown threat actors launched a sophisticated global phishing campaign targeting employees of 30 companies across 12 industries, distributing over 200 malicious links designed to steal user login credentials, with stolen credentials sent to attackers in real time via C2 servers or Telegram bots. Cybersecurity researchers at Group-IB reported on 11 December 2024. 
The industries targeted in this phishing operation included Energy, Fashion, Finance, Aerospace, Manufacturing, Telecommunications, Government, IT, Healthcare, Technology, and Entertainment. 
This cam

name: Unknown Hackers Disrupted the Network of the Portuguese Agency for Administrative Modernization (AMA) with Ransomware Beginning on 10 October 2024
description: Beginning on 10 October 2024, unknown hackers disrupted the Portuguese Agency for Administrative Modernization (AMA) network with ransomware. Parts of the network were still inaccessible five days later.

name: Puerto Rico Senate
description: Puerto Rico’s Senate announced Wednesday that it was the target of a cyberattack that disabled its internet provider, phone system and official online page, the latest in a string of similar incidents in recent years.

name: Mayor's office of the Puerto Rican municipality of Rio Grande was hit by a cyber attack that was detected in November 2023
description:
The mayor's office of the Puerto Rican municipality of Río Grande suffered a cyberattack in November 2023. On 27 November, the municipality's IT manager noticed that no data was being displayed on the monitors and that administrative data had been extracted.
Forensic analysis by a private IT company confirmed that criminals had disrupted the municipality's online portal and encrypted administrative systems.
The damage to the system was estimated at approximately US$ 75,000.
Agents from the Criminal Investigations and Cybercrime Unit within the Puerto Rico's Department of Justice continue with

name: Unidentified threat actor executed ransomware attack on Puerto Nuevo Terminals Consortium beginning on 20 April 2024
description:
On 20 April 2024, an unidentified threat actor launched a ransomware attack on Puerto Nuevo Terminals, a major facility at the Port of San Juan, Puerto Rico, which is part of a consortium formed by Luis Ayala Colón and Puerto Rico Terminals. 
Puerto Rico Port Authority Director Joel Pizá Batiz pointed to increased traffic in the port area due to the incident at Puerto Nuevo Terminals. Despite ongoing efforts to restore normalcy, traffic on Kennedy Avenue in San Juan continues to be slow.
The ransomware attack caused temporary disruptions to terminal operations and posed challenges to operation

name: Adobe Zero Day Hack Qatar
description: Unknown Actors infected computers of the qatari foreign office with malware, via an Adobe zero-day

name: Unknown actors defaced the website of the Romanian Ministry of Education with an anti-school message on the night of 7-8 May 2023
description:
Unknown actors defaced the website of the Romanian Ministry of Education with an anti-system message on the night of 7-8 May 2023.
The hackers called on the country's students to stop attending school referring to it as a "waste of time".

name: Ransomware attack allegedly by Backmydata ransomware group on Romanian health IT provider affected operation of 25 Romanian hospitals in February 2024
description: In the night of 11-12 February 2024, a ransomware attack directed against the Hipocrate Information System, a platform for health services in Romania, affected at least 25 Romanian hospitals, including cancer treatment centres. Files and databases were encrypted, and the targeted hospitals disconnected their networks to isolate compromised systems. Due to the disruption the hospitals had to revert to paper records. An additional 75 institutions disconnected from their systems from the platform as a precautionary measure. The Directoratul Național de Securitate Cibernetică (DNSC), the Romanian

name: Operation Pawn Storm 2007
description: Fancy Bear attacked the military and defense contractors of the US and some of their allies in a longterm espionage campaign, with the usage of some Zerodays

name: Attor Spyplatform
description: Unknown actors developed an spyplatform that managed to misuse various sites in the Russian language space, to force an targeted espionage campaign

name: Anonymous DDOS on Kremlin Round 2
description: Anonymous Russia likely launched a powerful DDoS attack that temporarily knocked out websites belonging to the Kremlin, the Russian central bank, and Foreign Ministry. It is unknown if this is related to the war in Ukraine, but in their first round of DDoS attacks on the Kremlin, the attack was considered a response to Russian censorship.

name: Russia economic espionage
description: 20 Russian government and military facilities alongside several defence contractors were targeted via a spear-phishing campaign with malware sent via email.

name: Attack on Russian Central Election Commission
description: The Russian Central Election Commission was hit by a DDoS attack "from 15 different countries".

name: Bitcoin Scam
description: On September 2, 2021, unknown actors hacked the website of the administration of the Russian city of Ryazan. In a first post, the hackers wrote on the website that users of an application would receive a certain amount of Bitcoins. In the second post, a Bitcoin lottery was advertised on the website.

name: Cyber-espionage group Cloud Atlas gained acess to various sectors across countries in Eastern Europe beginning in March 2022
description:
Cyber-espionage group Cloud Atlas gained acess to various sectors in various countries in Eastern Europe beginning in March 2022, according to technical reports of Check Point Research and Russia-based Positive Technologies. (The US Treasury Department sanctioned Positive Technology on 21 April 2021 over the company's alleged support to the FSB.) 
From March to April 2022, Cloud Atlas targeted the pro-Russian breakaway state Transnistrian Moldavian Republic.
Starting in June 2022, Cloud Atlas targeted the transportation and military radio-electronics sector in Belarus; the government, energy a

name: The websites of several Russian ministries and agencies were defaced with reference to the Russian-Ukrainian war on March 8th 2022
description: The websites of several Russian ministries and agencies were defaced leaving an apparently anti-war image on the given main page on March 8th 2022.  The target list include the following entities: websites of the Energy Ministry, the Federal State Statistics Service, the Federal Penitentiary Service, the Federal Bailiff Service, the Federal Antimonopoly Service, the Culture Ministry, and other Russian state agencies. Russian Ministry of Economic Development and Russian Digital Development Ministry confirmed the incident two news outlets.

name: Several Russian Airlines have been targeted with DDoS attacks in May 2022
description: Several Russian Airlines say that they have been targeted with DDoS attacks in May 2022. Among the targeted airlines have been Rossiya, Aurora, ALROSA, and others.

name: Unknown hackers destroyed data in networks of Russian city halls and courts using new malware CryWiper in the fall of 2022
description:
Unknown hackers disguised the previously unknown destructive CryWiper malware as ransomware with the intention to delete data in networks of Russian city halls and courts in the fall of 2022, according to Kaspersky in a Russian blogpost. 
Igor Bederov, IT-security expert at T.Hunter, told the Russian newspaper Izvestia that this cyber incident is shaped by the current geopolitical context in which foreign hackers are encouraged to attack Russian targets.

name: Unknown actors targeted Zimbra-using organizations worldwide with phishing campaign since April 2023
description: Unknown actors have targeted Zimbra-using organizations in a worldwide phishing campaign since April 2023, according to a report by ESET from 17 August 2023. The reported targets were small and medium-sized businesses and government entities, mainly from Poland, Ecuador, and Italy. ESET stated that the targets were connected by their use of Zimbra. Additionally, affected countries included Russia, Kazakhstan, Ukraine, France, Mexico, Brazil, Argentina, Chile, and Peru. According to ESET, it seems likely that the attackers were able to compromise the victims' administrator accounts, allowing th

name: Unknown actors disrupted information system of a dispensary of the Russian pharmacy JSC in the Primorsky Krai region on 14 July 2023
description:
Unknown actors disrupted the information system of a dispensary of the Russian pharmacy JSC in the Primorsky Krai region on 14 July 2023, the local health minister Anastasia Khudchenko reported on her Telegram channel the same day.
Khudchenko announced that the distribution of subsidised medicine had to be suspended as a result of the incident. Manual operations would ensure the continued distribution of urgently needed medication.

name: Unknown hackers disrupt services of Russian supermarket chain Verny on 1 June 2024
description:
Unknown hackers have disrupted the website and mobile app of Russian supermarket chain Verny on the weekend of 1 June 2024. 
Due to the attack, Verny supermarkets were not able to process payments via bank cards or fulfill online orders.
The company's director did not confirm whether a ransom was demanded.
Local security experts draw comparisons to an incident affecting the Russian delivery service CDEK a week earlier. However, it is unclear whether the same hacker group is responsible for the targeting of Verny.
Verny is one of Russias largest supermarket chains, with a revenue of around $1.3

name: Unknown actor conducted an DDoS attack on Russia's Kursk region on 8 August 2024
description: An unknown actor conducted an DDoS attack on Russia's Kursk region on 8 August 2024. The unnamed hackers targeted government and business websites, as well as critical infrastructure services, making some of them temporarily unavailable, Russian state media reported. This incident coincided with Ukrainian military advancements in the Kursk region.

name: Unknown hackers disrupted a Russian telecommunications operator on 21 August 2024
description:
Unknown hackers disrupted a Russian telecommunications operator on 21 August 2024, the Federal Service for Supervision of Communications, Information Technology and Mass Media of Russia (Roskomnadzor, RKN) reported on the same day.
