The head of the OSBA told news media that the association typically does not store any personal information like financial information or social security numbers. As of now, there is no reason to believe that any sensitive data from members has been compromised.
She also ruled

name: Unknown Hacker Gained Control of US-Based Unicoin's Google G-Suite Accounts and Locked Out Employees on 9 August 2024
description:
On 9 August 2024, a hacker gained unauthorized access to the Google G-Suite accounts of San Francisco-based cryptocurrency firm Unicoin, locking all employees out of key systems, including G-Mail and G-Drive, for nearly four days. The breach disrupted operations until 13 August 2024, when Unicoin regained control of its accounts.
While no evidence of stolen digital assets has been found, the breach led to discrepancies in the personal data of employees and contractors, particularly within the accounting department. Additionally, traces of hacked messages were identified in certain managers' em

name: Unknown threat actors conducted ransomware attack on the internal network of the US city of Flint on 14 August 2024
description: Unknown threat actors conducted a ransomware attack on the internal network of the US city of Flint on 14 August 2024. Due to the attack, several services are currently unavailable such as the online or credit card payments of water, sewer, and taxes. E-Mail and phone services are also experiencing gaps in service. Whether internal data such as resident or employee personal data was accessed is still under investigation.

name: Unknown hackers targeted Microchip Technology Inc. (USA) with Cyberattack on 17 August 2024
description:
Unknown hackers targeted Microchip Technology Inc. (USA) with a cyberattack. The company, which supplies chips to the US defense industry, detected suspicious activity on its information technology systems on August 17, 2024. It was later confirmed that an unauthorized party had breached certain servers and disrupted some business operations. Upon discovering the breach, Microchip implemented measures to contain the incident, including isolating compromised systems, shutting down certain operations, and launching an investigation with external experts.
The company’s manufacturing facilities ar

name: Unknown Threat Actors Breached the Data of 77,099 Fidelity Investments Customers in Maine, United States, Obtaining Personal Information and Creating Two New Customer Accounts Between 17 and 19 August
description: Unknown threat actors compromised the data of 77,099 Fidelity Investments customers in Maine between 17 and 19 August 2024, obtaining personal information, including Social Security numbers and driver’s licenses. They also created two new customer accounts. Fidelity disclosed the breach in a notification letter sent to thousands of customers on 9 October 2024 and offered affected individuals credit monitoring and identity restoration services.

name: Unknown Hackers Breached McDonald’s Instagram Account in $700K Solana-Based Crypto Scam on 21 August 2024
description:
On 21 August 2024, unknown hackers gained control of McDonald’s official Instagram account and the personal X account of the company's senior markeing director. The hackers used this access to promote a fraudulent Solana-based cryptocurrency scheme. The attackers posted links to a fake "Grimace" meme coin, tricking followers into transferring funds through the Solana blockchain, believing they were investing in a McDonald’s-branded cryptocurrency. The scam resulted in over $700,000 in stolen funds.
McDonald’s quickly regained control of its Instagram account and removed the fraudulent posts. T

name: Unknown threat actors breached the network of American Neighborhood Mortgage Acceptance Company LLC's (AnnieMac) beginning on 21 August 2024
description: Between 21 August 2024, and 23 August 2024, Unknown Threat Actors gained access to American Neighborhood Mortgage Acceptance Company LLC's (AnnieMac) network, potentially viewing and/or copying files containing sensitive personal information, including names and Social Security numbers, of 171,074 customers.

name: Unknown attackers gained access to the systems of US oilfield services firm Halliburton around 21 August 2024
description:
Unknown attackers gained access to the systems of US oilfield services firm Halliburton around 21 August 2024. 
The company, headquartered in North Houston, Texas, confirmed that a third party managed to access internal systems according to an SEC filing from 23 August. 
The firm plays a major role in the oil and natural gas supply chain, including the maintenance of oil production facilities. According to Reuters, the incident impacted systems on the company's North Houston campus, as well as global connectivity networks. 
In November 2024, Halliburton announced that the ransomware attack had

name: Criminal Groups IntelBroker and EnergyWeaponUser claim attack on semiconductor company AMD on 28 August 2024
description:
The criminal groups IntelBroker and EnergyWeaponUser claimed to have targeted the semiconductor company AMD on 28 August 2024.
The stolen data includes user credentials, internal resolutions, and detailed case descriptions.
AMD has said that it is aware of the claims made by the cybercriminal organization and is working with law enforcement and a third-party hosting partner to investigate the situation and is also assessing the extent of the data breach.
This breach follows a similar incident in June 2024, where the company's employee data was compromised.

name: Unknown threat actors launched a ransomware attack on Great Plains Regional Medical Center in the United States beginning on 5 September 2024
description: On September 5-8, 2024, unknown threat actors launched a ransomware attack on Great Plains Regional Medical Center in Oklahoma, compromising personal data of 133,000 individuals, including sensitive information such as name, demographic information, health insurance information, clinical treatment information, drivers license number, and/or Social Security number. The attackers accessed and encrypted files on the systems and copied some of them. The incident was reported to the US Department of Health and Human Services by the Oklahoma Medical Center. The organization quickly restored its syst

name: Unknown threat actors attacked MoneyGram in September 2024 and stole data
description:
Unknown threat actors attacked MoneyGram in September 2024 affecting several systems. After several customers complaints about the lack of services since Friday 20 September, the company itself confirmed a cybersecurity incident. Due to the attack they shut their systems down, impacting network connectivity. Several services like the MoneyGram App or sending and receiving payments are currently unavailable.
MoneyGram released a statement on October 8, 2024, saying that an unknown party gained unauthorized access and stole personal information from September 20 to 22, 2024. This included names,

name: Unknown threat actors launched a ransomware attack on the Houston Housing Authority in the United States on 21 September 2024, encrypting the agency's virtual servers
description: Unknown Threat Actors launched a ransomware attack on the Houston Housing Authority (HHA) on 21 September 2024, encrypting the agency's virtual servers. Although the HHA's firewall initially appeared to have thwarted data theft, the hackers later claimed to have obtained sensitive data from the agency. The HHA's IT director, Roy Spivey, and board chair, Jody Proler, assessed the agency's security vulnerabilities and formed an action plan, which included a $311,000 expenditure to bolster network security, adding two-factor authentication on all desktop servers, enhanced firewalls, more robust f

name: Unknown Threat Actors compromised the water treatment facility of Arkansas City, Kansas, USA, via ransomware attack on Sunday, 22 September 2024, forcing the facility to switch to manual operations
description: Unknown Threat Actors launched a ransomware attack on the Arkansas City water treatment facility in Kansas, USA, on Sunday, 22 September 2024, forcing the facility to switch to manual operations. The attackers, believed to be a ransomware group, successfully encrypted at least some computer systems and likely stole information from the water facility, with an apparent ransom note appearing on a water treatment facility computer demanding the employee contact the attackers via email. The city has notified relevant authorities, including Homeland Security and FBI agents, who are investigating th

name: Unknown Threat Actors Stole Sensitive Healthcare Information of 422,424 People From American Addiction Centers in the United States Using Ransomware Between 23 and 26 September 2024
description: On 23-26 September 2024, American Addiction Centers was breached by a ransomware attack, resulting in the exposure of sensitive healthcare information of 422,424 people. The attack was discovered on 26 September 2024. The stolen data included Social Security numbers, health insurance information, names, addresses, phone numbers, and medical record numbers. The company mailed out breach notification letters to the affected individuals ahead of the Christmas holiday. Breach notices were filed in Texas, where over 26,000 people were impacted, and California.

name: Unknown hackers launched a ransomware attack on American UMC Health System on September 26, 2024
description: Unknown hackers launched a ransomware attack on Lubbock, Texas-based UMC Health System, a level 1 trauma centre from the US, on September 26, 2024, causing the system to disconnect its systems due to unusual activity and resulting in the diversion of emergency and non-emergency patients to other local healthcare facilities. Furthermore, it caused the interruption of the organization's phones and patient portal, with the health system enlisting third-party help in its response and investigation, and no timeline for restoration, as reported on the health system's website on September 27, 2024.

name: Unknown Threat Actor Stole Personal and Health Data of 1,060,936 Patients from Community Health Center in the United States Using a Network Breach Between Mid-October 2024 and 2 January 2025
description: On 2 January 2025, Community Health Center (CHC), a US healthcare provider, discovered that a skilled criminal hacker had gained access to its network in mid-October 2024, resulting in a data breach that impacted the personal and health data of 1,060,936 patients. The hacker stole files containing patients' personal and health information, including names, dates of birth, addresses, phone numbers, emails, Social Security numbers, medical diagnoses, treatment details, test results, and health insurance information. CHC reported the breach to Maine's attorney general and hired investigators to a

name: Unknown Threat Actors Caused a Network Outage at Bartow County School System in the US State of Georgia During October 2024
description: Unknown threat actors caused a network outage at the Bartow County School System in Georgia, USA, taking down its websites, online access and email, affecting around 14,000 students, in October 2024. Recovery efforts by the school district remained ongoing as of 18 October 2024, according to Alisha Evans, Bartow communications coordinator.

name: An unknown actor disrupted the network of the Wayne County government in Michigan on 2 October 2024
description:
An unknown actor disrupted the network of the Wayne County government in Michigan on 2 October 2024, WXYZ reported the next day, citing unspecified sources.
The unspecified source said the hackers made a financial demand via ransomware.

name: Unknown Threat Actors breached the billing systems of American Water Works Company in the United States on October 3, 2024
description: Unknown Threat Actors compromised American Water Works Company, a New Jersey-based company responsible for providing water to more than 14 million people in 14 states, in a cyberattack on October 3, 2024, which resulted in the loss of billing systems, according to a Securities and Exchange Commission filing. American Water Works Company disconnected and deactivated certain systems as part of the incident response procedure. The company's water and wastewater facilities and operations remain unaffected by the incident. American Water Works Company notified law enforcement about the attack and i

name: Unknown threat actors compromised the company network of Maxar Space Systems on 4 October 2024
description: On 4 October 2024, Hackers breached U.S. satellite maker Maxar Space Systems and accessed personal data belonging to its employees, including name, social security number and the employee number. The company informs in a notification to impacted individuals. On 11 October 2024, immediately after discovering the unauthorized access, the company took action to prevent the hackers from reaching further into the system. The company said: “Our information security team discovered that a hacker using a Hong Kong-based IP address targeted and accessed a Maxar system containing certain files with empl

name: Unknown Threat Actors Launched a Cyberattack on Winnebago Public School District in the United States from 21 to 23 October 2024
description: Unknown threat actors compromised the Winnebago Public School district in Northeast Nebraska, disrupting their phone and internet services, on Monday, 21 October 2024, forcing the district to cancel classes on Wednesday, 23 October 2024, and send students home early on Tuesday, 22 October 2024, with parent-teacher conferences still scheduled for Thursday, 24 October 2024, from 2 p.m. to 6 p.m., KTIV disclosed in a report on 23 October 2024.

name: Unknown Threat Actors Breached the City of McKinney Government Systems in Texas, Exposing Sensitive Information of 17,751 Residents On 31 October 2024
description: On 31 October 2024, the city of McKinney, Texas, was breached by a cyberattack, which was discovered on 14 November 2024. The attackers gained unauthorized access to the city's government systems, exposing sensitive information of 17,751 residents, including names, addresses, Social Security numbers, drivers license numbers, credit card information, financial account data, and medical insurance information. The city's IT team severed any unauthorized activity and contacted the FBI, Department of Homeland Security, and Texas Department of Information. The city began sending breach notification

name: Unknown threat actors breached the City of Sheboygan network in the United States  on or around November 1, 2024
description: On or around 1 November 2024, unknown threat actors breached the confidentiality and integrity of the City of Sheboygan's network through an external, unauthorized access. The attackers left a ransom message. The City of Sheboygan secured the network and started a forensic review. Local cybersecurity expert Brian Collins, co-founder of PanaTech, commented on the attack, stating that the city likely lacked active monitoring of network activity and that it is harder to identify when someone has breached firewalls nowadays. The city stated that it had not discovered evidence that any sensitive pe

name: Unknown threat actors breached Byte Federal by exploiting a GitLab vulnerability on November 18, 2024
description:
On November 18, 2024, US Bitcoin ATM operator Byte Federal became aware that unknown threat actors gained access to one of their servers, exploiting a GitLab vulnerability, potentially compromising the personal information of 58,000 customers, including name, birthdate, address, phone number, email address, government-issued ID, social security number, transaction activity, and photographs of users. 
Byte Federal disclosed the data breach and responded by shutting down its platform, locking out the attacker, and securing the compromised server.

name: Unknown threat actors hacked Palo Alto Networks firewalls in the United States using zero-day vulnerabilities in November 2024
description: Over 2,000 Palo Alto Networks firewalls were hacked in November 2024, exploiting recently patched zero-day vulnerabilities CVE-2024-0012 and CVE-2024-9474 in PAN-OS. The attackers gained administrator privileges and deployed web shells on compromised devices, granting persistent remote access. Palo Alto Networks and their Unit 42 tracked the exploitation activity, and Shadowserver researchers reported that approximately 2,000 devices were hacked, primarily in the US (554) and India (461), followed by Thailand (80), Mexico (48), Indonesia (43), Turkey (41), the U.K. (39), Peru (36), and South A

name: Unknown threat actors launched a ransomware attack against medical devices producer Artivion on 21 November 2024
description:
On 21 November 2024 at the latest, unknown threat actors launched a ransomware attack on Artivion, a leading manufacturer of cardiac surgery devices. The attack encrypted some of its systems and stole data from compromised systems. The company took certain systems offline, initiated an investigation, and engaged external advisors to assess, contain, and remediate the incident. The attack disrupted Artivion's corporate operations, order processing, and shipping, but most disruptions have been addressed. 
Artivion disclosed the incident in a Monday 8-K filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchang

name: Unknown Cyber Criminals Compromised Financial Transactions of White Lake Township in Michigan, USA on 21 November 2024
description: On 21 November 2024, unknown threat actors carried out a sophisticated cyberattack on White Lake Township Hall in Oakland County, Michigan, compromising the township's financial transactions and impacting a new issue of infrastructure bonds related to a 50 million USD  civic center project. The attack, which was detected on the same day, has put the project on hold. White Lake Township Police Chief Daniel Keller and Township Supervisor Rik Kowall informed the public about the attack. The incident is under active investigation by federal authorities and impacted financial institutions, who are

name: Unknown threat actors launched a ransomware attack on ENGlobal Corporation, encrypting some of its data files on 25 November 2024
description:
On 25 November 2024, Unknown Threat Actors launched a ransomware attack against ENGlobal Corporation, a major energy industry contractor, by illegally accessing and encrypting some of its data files, limiting employee access to its IT system and hindering operations. ENGlobal Corporation reported the incident to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, has started an internal investigation, and has hired external cybersecurity experts to address the issue.
On January 27, 2025, ENGlobal Corporation updated its first report and added that the incident disrupted access to key business applica

name: Unknown threat actors launched a ransomware attack on the city of Hoboken, New Jersey (USA) on 27 November 2024
description: On 27 November 2024, an unknown threat actor launched a ransomware attack on the city of Hoboken, causing widespread issues and service shutdowns ahead of the Thanksgiving holiday. The attack resulted in the suspension of online city services, cancellation of municipal court and street sweeping, but waste collection and recreational programs continued. The Hoboken Police Department, City administration, and IT department are investigating the attack. No group has claimed responsibility for the incident.

name: Unknown Threat Actors Disrupted Online Ordering Operations Of Krispy Kreme In The United States By Breaching Its Information Technology Systems On 29 November 2024
description: On 29 November 2024, unknown threat actors compromised the confidentiality and availability of Krispy Kreme's information technology systems, disrupting online ordering across the United States. Krispy Kreme, which operates 1,521 shops and 15,800 points of access, detected unauthorized activity and engaged leading cybersecurity experts to address the incident. The breach has materially impacted the company's operations, with anticipated financial losses due to reduced digital sales, fees for cybersecurity experts, and system restoration costs. The company's stock price dropped by 2% following

name: Unknown threat actors gained access to and encrypted the servers of water treatment company Kurita America in Minnesota, USA beginning on 29 November 2024
description:
Unknown threat actors launched a ransomware attack on Kurita America, a US subsidiary of Japanese water treatment company Kurita Water Industries, headquartered in Minnesota. This resulted in the encryption of multiple servers, potentially leaking customer, business partner, and employee data to third parties. On 29 November 2024, an alert from the security systems triggered the investigation.
The main servers were already restored, and business operations were not disrupted, as reported by the company on 7 December 2024.

name: Unknown threat actors launched a ransomware attack on U.S. subsidiary of the Japanese tea producer Ito En on 2 December 2024
description: The Japanese corporation Ito En confirmed on 6 December 2024, that its U.S. subsidiary was hit with ransomware. The company is the largest producer of green tea in Japan and has subsidiaries in the U.S., Australia, China and Indonesia. The company said a third party attacked Ito En North America as they discovered on 2 December 2024, that some file servers were encrypted. The company said the file servers have been isolated from the network and are currently being restored using backup data from before the attack. Additionally, the information leak is being investigated.

name: Unknown Threat Actors Launched a Ransomware Attack on PIH Health Network and IT Systems in California on 4 December 2024
description: On 4 December 2024, PIH Health, a California hospital network, was breached by a ransomware attack launched by unknown threat actors, disrupting its network and IT systems across multiple facilities, including PIH Health Downey Hospital, PIH Health Good Samaritan Hospital, PIH Health Whittier Hospital, urgent care centers, physicians' offices, home health, and hospice services. Despite the attack, urgent care centers and emergency rooms remained operational. Critical systems such as patient health records, laboratory, pharmacy, radiology, patient registration, and other IT tools were taken off

name: Unidentified Cyber Criminals Breached Rhode Island’s RIBridges System in the US, Exposing Personal Data of Benefit Applicants and Recipients Starting on 5 December 2024
description:
On 5 December 2024, unknown perpetrators targeted Rhode Island's public benefits system, RIBridges, managed by Deloitte, in a ransomware attack that compromised personal and financial data. The breach, confirmed on 10 December, exposed sensitive information, including names, addresses, birth dates, Social Security numbers, and bank details of individuals who applied for or received benefits such as Medicaid, SNAP, and TANF.
The state proactively shut down RIBridges on 13 December after discovering malicious code, halting the system's operations to contain the threat. Although no identity theft

name: Unknown threat actors breached US education IT system provider PowerSchool on 28 December 2024
description: On December 28, 2024, Unknown Threat Actors breached the student information system vendor PowerSchool, affecting multiple school boards in the US and Canada. The attackers accessed names, birth dates, phone numbers, and home mailing addresses of students and families through PowerSchool's community-focused customer support portal, PowerSource. PowerSchool immediately engaged its cybersecurity response protocols and informed law enforcement. The company assured that no financial information was accessed, login information was not impacted, and the incident was contained. PowerSchool also state

name: Unknown threat actors breached Matagorda County government in Texas starting on 24 January 2025
description: On 27 January 2025, Matagorda County government in Texas, serving about 40,000 residents, suffered a cyberattack that forced officials to declare a disaster. The attack, discovered on 24 January 2025, involved unauthorized access to internal systems, impacting various departments and disrupting some operations. With assistance from cybersecurity experts, the Department of Public Safety Cybersecurity Division, the Texas Department of Emergency Management, and the Department of Informational Services, the breach was contained to internal county systems. The FBI was notified of the incident. Mata

name: Unknown Threat Actors Launched a Ransomware Attack on New York Blood Center Enterprises in the United States on 26 January 2025
description: On 26 January 2025, New York Blood Center Enterprises (NYBCe) was hit by a ransomware attack, disrupting critical blood donation services across the US. The attack was identified and confirmed by NYBCe and third-party cybersecurity experts. Law enforcement was notified. No information was provided about the identity of the perpetrator or whether any data was accessed. The incident was disclosed by NYBCe in a statement on 29 January 2025. New York State Senator Dean Murray also informed the public about the attack and its impact on a scheduled blood drive on 30 January 2025. NYBCe provides bloo

name: Unknown Threat Actors Breached West Virginia's Harrison County School District in the United States on 27 January 2025
description: On 27 January 2025, West Virginia's Harrison County school district was breached by a cyber attack, upon which the district shut down its network, however, it did not disclose whether any data was exfiltrated.

name: Unknown threat actors breached Appomattox County Public Schools in Virginia, United States on 19 February 2025
description: Networks of Appomattox County Public Schools and Appomattox County, Virginia  were breached by an unknown entity in the week of 18 February 2025. The incident was discovered and reported by the school district and county. Outside cybersecurity experts were contacted to help secure their systems and investigate the cause. The incident resulted in the closure of Appomattox County Public Schools on Tuesday and the remainder of the week due to winter weather. The county and school district enaged in collaboration with state and federal authorities on the investigation.

name: Unknown actor(s) infiltrated the FBI New York Field Office in February 2023
description: Unknown actor(s) hacked into the FBI's field office in New York in February 2023, attacking a system used to investigate child exploitation.

name: Unknown actors disrupted the networks of the US city of Oak Ridge in Tennessee in March 2023
description: Unknown actors disrupted the networks of the US city of Oak Ridge in Tennessee with ransomware in March 2023, the city announced via Twitter on 22 March 2023.

name: Unknown actors breached network of Village of Skokie in Illinois and stole data
description: Unknown actors breached the network of the village of Skokie in the US state of Illinois and stole certain files, the information platform Patch.com reported based on statements from employees involved. On 18 December 2023, the village administration's IT department asked employees to turn off all computers in response to what was described as a village-wide network outage. On 21 December, the assistant village manager informed employees that an intrusion by unauthorized external actors led to the network outage. In an update from 9 January 2024, the assistant village manager confirmed the the

name: Unknown threat actors accessed data of US Government Accountability Office through Atlassian vulnerability at contractor CGI Federal
description: On 17 January 2024, CGI Federal, a contractor of the US Government Accountability Office (GAO), notified the agency that some of its data was compromised through a vulnerability in an Atlassian workforce collaboration tool. The data breach affected records from 2007 to 2017 related to approximately 6,600 people, the majority of which are current and former GAO employees, in addition to information about GAO's businesses dealings. Compromised information included names, social security numbers, addresses, and some banking details.

name: American Express Card Member data exposed through data breach at third-party merchant processor in the beginning of March 2024
description: As disclosed in a data breach notice to their customers by American Express, a global provider of financial services based in the US, a merchant processor tasked with handling American Express Card member data was compromised in the beginning of March 2024. Information of American Express card members, including names, account numbers and expiration dates, may have been exposed.

name: Unknown actors exfiltrated data from US-medical device company OraSure Technology in 2024
description:
According to a Form 8-K filling to the United States Security and Exchange Commission (SEC) from 10 April 2024, US medical device company OraSure Technology was affected by a cyber incident, detected by the company around 27 March 2027. OraSure further stated that "an unauthorized third party gained access to company data from certain information systems". At the time of the reporting, it was not clear which kind of information was affected. However, according to OraSure, its core financial and operational systems remained unaffected. 
Amongst others, OraSure developed the OraQuick test, which

name: Attack on Al-Qaida Comm-Systems
description: Communication networks of Al Qaida are disrupted for severeal days by unknown hacker.

name: Hacker disrupt AlQaida Forums
description: Three Al-Qaida forums are disrupted by DDoS attacks from anonymous attackers.

name: DDOS vs. Wikileaks
description: WikiLeaks hit with'targeted' cyberattack after publishing over 8,000 more DNC emails

name: Unknown hackers hijacked and defaced website of Uruguayan Institute for Children and Adolescents (INAU) on 26 April 2024
description:
On April 26, 2024, the official website of the Uruguayan Institute for Children and Adolescents (INAU) was defaced by unidentified threat actors to promote an online casino, RudalToto, which operates from Indonesia. The unauthorised content modifications were aimed at drawing visitors of the INAU website to the betting platform.
Upon noticing the breach, the INAU responded by engaging its Information and Communication Technology (ICT) division in collaboration with the web hosting provider. 
Efforts were focused on restoring the website to its original state using previous backups, a process e

name: Unknown hackers target Uruguayan municipality of Paysandu with ransomware attack on 24 July 2024
description:
Unknown hackers target Uruguayan municipality Paysandu with ransomware on 24 July 2024.
The municipality of Paysandú suffered a ransomware attack last Wednesday in which it lost ‘all information’ and rendered several services of the departmental government inoperable, as confirmed by Mayor Nicolás Olivera. Cyber criminals have managed to penetrate the municipality's systems using encryption ransomware and are now demanding a ransom worth 9,000 bitcoins, i.e. around $610,000.
The mayor said that they are working intensively with cyber experts to investigate and restore operations. Communication

name: An unknown attacker manipulates the website of the Uruguayan National Party on 5 August 2024
description: A group of hackers redirected visitors of the website of the National Party of Uruguay to a website controled by the attackers that showed a statement threatening Senator Graciela Bianchi. The actors further claimed to possess compromising WhatsApp messages from the senator. According to Macarena Rubio, president of the board of directors of the National Party (PN), the political party filed a complaint with the Cybercrime Unit of the police. According to the statements of the nationalist leader, the objective of the action is to be able to “identify those responsible”. The incident was also a

name: Unnamed hacker group targeted telecom and government sectors in Asia since 2021
description: Unnamed hacker group was targeting Telecommunications and Government organizations in Kazakhstan, Uzbekistan, Pakistan, and Vietnam since 2021. Security firm Check Point tracked related tools under the campaign 'Staying Alive'. While not sharing any clear overlaps with known actors, the tools showed connections to a set of infrastructure previously tied by Kaspersky to an actor tracked as ToddyCat. Kaspersky identified ToddyCat as China-affiliated with an activity profile that matches the operating region Check Point observed for Staying Alive.

name: The hacker group Lazy Koala compromised 867 employee accounts of six Commonwealth of Independent States (CIS)
description:
The threat actor compromised 867 employee accounts of organisations across six Commonwealth of Independent States (CIS), the Russian IT security company Positive Technologies reported on 4 April 2024.
The affected CIS countries included Russia, Belarus, Uzbekistan, Kyrgyzstan, Tajikistan, and Armenia. The affected targets included government, financial, healthcare and educational institutions. 
The group likely intended to leverage the gained access to exploit it for subsequent hacks or selling it to other threat actors.

name: Unknown attackers disrupted the technological platform SICA of Venezuelan National Superintendence of Agri-Food Management (SUNAGRO) in January 2023
description: Unknown attackers disrupted the technological platform SICA (Sistema Integral De Control Agroalimentario) which monitors the production chain of agricultural produce and is operated by the Venezuelan National Superintendence of Agri-Food Management (SUNAGRO), during 11-13 January 2023, according to SUNAGRO.

name: Unknown threat actors targeted the website of the Venezuelan National Assembly on 16 August 2024
description: Unknown threat actors targeted the website of the Venezuelan National Assembly on 16 August 2024. Due to the attack, the website is currently not accessible and official notes and communiqués will be disseminated on the Telegram messaging platform as an interim measure. According to the minister of science and technology, at least 106 government agencies have been targets of the digital attacks since the presidential elections on 28 July.

name: Venezuelan National Electoral Council allegedly hit by DDoS attack 26 August 2024
description:
The Venezuelan National Electoral Council (CNE) was allegedly hit by a DDoS attack on 26 August 2024, which impacted the availability of its website and related services. According to the state-run Venezuelan Television (VTV) channel, the attack took place against the centres which host the website of the CNE and was tracked to IP addresses outside of Venezuela, though it was unclear where exactly the attack took place from. 
The attack follows a previous alleged attack on the CNE earlier in August. Prior to this new attack, the CNE's website had been reactivated in preparation to publish the

name: Vietnam Ministry Hack
description: Malware has been specifically crafted for the systems used by the employees at the Vietnamese Ministry of Natural Resources and Environment

name: SignSight
description: Private companies and government agencies in Vietnam and Philippines attacked during "Operation SignSight".

name: Unnamed hacker group targeted telecom and government sectors in Asia since 2021
description: Unnamed hacker group was targeting Telecommunications and Government organizations in Kazakhstan, Uzbekistan, Pakistan, and Vietnam since 2021. Security firm Check Point tracked related tools under the campaign 'Staying Alive'. While not sharing any clear overlaps with known actors, the tools showed connections to a set of infrastructure previously tied by Kaspersky to an actor tracked as ToddyCat. Kaspersky identified ToddyCat as China-affiliated with an activity profile that matches the operating region Check Point observed for Staying Alive.

name: Unidentified threat actor targeted Vietnamese government entity using Zimbra vulnerability in July 2023
description: Prior to Zimbra's official patch release for CVE-2023-37580 on 25 July that seals a vulnerability in a web client for the company's email servers, Google's Threat Analysis Group (TAG) observed an unidentified party taking advantage of the vulnerability to execute a phishing attack aimed at seizing login details of a Vietnamese government body. According to the report from 16 November, TAG analysts observed an exploit web address directed to a program that showed a fake webpage to obtain users' webmail login details, then shared the stolen details with a link hosted on a government domain that

name: PetroVietnam Oil Corporation (PVOIL) targeted by ransomware on 2 April 2024
description: The Vietnamese PetroVietnam Oil Corporation (PVOIL) was targeted by ransomware on 2 April 2024, according to a company statement from the same day. The incident led to the disruption of the company's information technology system, which operates over 780 petrol stations nationwide, including its electronic invoicing system. As a result, electronic invoicing for PV Oil sales was temporarily suspended. PVOIL reported the incident to authorities and is working with the Ministry of Public Security to remediate the incident. To ensure the supply of petrol, PV Oil and its member units continue to se

name: Unknown Perpetrators Hit Vietnamese State-Owned Postal Service Provider Vietnam Post With Ransomware Starting on 4 June 2024
description:
Vietnam Post, the state-owned postal service provider, was hit by a ransomware attack on 4 June, causing significant disruption to its website and applications. The incident, which was detected in the morning, primarily affected postal delivery services, while postal finance, public administration and goods distribution were not affected.
In response, Vietnam Post activated emergency protocols, including the shutdown of IT systems, to isolate the intrusion and protect data.

name: Unknown Threat Actors Stole Credentials of Employees of 30 Companies Across 12 Industries Worldwide Beginning in Early July 2024
description:
Beginning in at least July 2024, unknown threat actors launched a sophisticated global phishing campaign targeting employees of 30 companies across 12 industries, distributing over 200 malicious links designed to steal user login credentials, with stolen credentials sent to attackers in real time via C2 servers or Telegram bots. Cybersecurity researchers at Group-IB reported on 11 December 2024. 
The industries targeted in this phishing operation included Energy, Fashion, Finance, Aerospace, Manufacturing, Telecommunications, Government, IT, Healthcare, Technology, and Entertainment. 
This cam

name: Unknown attackers disrupted the operations of the British Virgin Islands Electricity Corporation with ransomware on 19 August 2024
description:
Unknown attackers targeted the systems of the British Virgin Islands Electricity Corporation (BVIEC) with ransomware on 19 August 2024.
The attack was conducted on the heals of the tropical storm "Ernesto" which already put pressure on BVIEC to restore damaged parts of the electricity grid. 
According to the General Manager of BVIEC, Dr. Neil Smith, ransomware of the Akira strand was used in the attack. The incident was discovered on 19 August. Further investigations revealed that the initial intrusion took place several months earlier. 
The attack affected internal as well as external operati

name: AR Bunse
description: A single threat actor used the Pakistani front company Bunse Technologies to send malware to targets in Afghanistan and India using RTF documents with political and governmental themes. They also exploited the CVE-2017-11882 vulnerability and targeted mobile devices.

name: The Pakistani-based threat actor Storm-0156 (also known as SideCopy or Transparent Tribe) compromised Afghan government networks beginning December 2022 at the latest
description:
The Pakistani-based threat actor Storm-0156 (also known as SideCopy or Transparent Tribe) compromised Afghan government networks between December 2022 and mid-2023, using CrimsonRAT and Waiscot malware to gain access to these systems. This campaign focused on espionage, collecting sensitive information from Afghan government entities. Storm-0156's activities align with its broader objective of targeting South Asia's government, military, and industrial systems for intelligence.
Additionally, in another cyber incident, the Russian state-sponsored hacking group Turla infiltrated the Pakistani th

name: APT36 use various malware against Indian govt employees in an extended campaign - 2021
description: A new campaign from the hacking group tracked as APT36, aka 'Transparent Tribe' or' Mythic Leopard,' has been discovered using new custom malware and entry vectors in attacks against the Indian government. The threat actors are known to utilize their malware of choice, CrimsonRAT (remote access trojan), during this campaign, in addition to new malware as they use various initial entry mechanisms in an attempt to diversify, remain undetected, and gain access to further systems. According to Cisco Talos, the campaign has been documented since June 2021 and uses inauthentic domains that mimic aut

name: Pakistani state-sponsored hacker group Transparent Tribe gained access to Indian government officials' computers using Linux malware Poseidon
description:
Pakistani state-sponsored hacker group Transparent Tribe gained access to Indian government officials' computers using Linux malware Poseidon, IT security firm Uptycs reported on 17 April 2023.
To do so, they used the Kavach two-factor authentication tool, which Indian government officials use to access their email inboxes, as a cover to deploy the Poseidon malware in the background.

name: Pakistani state-sponsored hacking group Transparent Tribe gained access to the Android mobile phones of 150 targeted people and stole information using the CapraRAT backdoor beginning in July 2022
description:
The Pakistani hacking group Transparent Tribe, also known as APT36, gained access to the Android mobile phones of 150 targeted people and stole information using the CapraRAT backdoor beginning in July 2022, according to Slovakian IT security company ESET. 
The victims are mainly Pakistani and Indian individuals with political and military connections. Further unspecified individuals, affected by the campaign, are based in Russia, Oman, and Egypt. 
The hacker group used a romance scam to get their targets to install the trojanized messaging apps MeetsApp and MeetUp and then spy on their target

name: Pakistani APT group SideCopy gained access to India's Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO)
description: The Pakistan-based APT group SideCopy gained access to India's Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO), according to a technical report by US IT security company Cyble. Subordinated to India's Ministry of Defence, DRDO is mandated to research and develop defence applications for the armed forces.

name: Pakistani hacktivists 'Team Insane PK' disrupted websites of Delhi and Mumbai Police on 8 September 2023
description: Pakistani hacktivists 'Team Insane PK' disrupted the websites of the police departments of Delhi and Mumbai as well as other Indian organizations on 8 September 2023, the group announced on its Telegram channel on the same day. The DDoS attacks were launched on the eve of the G20 summit hosted under the Indian presidency in New Delhi during 9-10 September. Efforts by the group to take down the website of the Delhi Police continued until 9 September, resulting in a brief disruption of access for around half an hour.

description:
The Philippine Maritime Industry Authority (MARINA) has fallen victim to a breach by the hacker operating under the pseudonym "ph1ns". Data of shipowners and Filipino seafarers were exposed. Ph1ns announced the compromise on Breach Forums and presented samples of the obtained data, including names, addresses, contact details and financial data.
Despite MARINA's claims that no stakeholder data was compromised, the hacker contests this assessment. Approximately 20 gigabytes of data were exfiltrated via a vulnerability in the unrestricted file upload.

name: LSETack down
description: Anonymous has crippled the website of the London Stock Exchange in a protest against the global financial system. Anonymous claims the incident was one of 67 successful attacks carried out on the websites of major institutions last month. The targets included the Swiss National Bank, the Central Bank of Venezuela and the Federal Reserve Bank of San Francisco.

Karnowski was supporting the formation of joint opposition lists for the upcoming parliamentary elections at that time. According to information obtained by Gazeta Wyborcza, Poland`s Central Anticorruption Bureau (CBA) logged into Karnowski's phone at least "a dozen times between November 2018 and March 2019". It hijacked their command and control servers to gain access to already compromised networks of the Afghan government. Turla deployed their malware tools, TwoDash and Statuezy. description: Members of REvil ransomware group stole personal information of customers of Australian private health insurance provider Medibank in October 2022. A week before the company became aware of the data theft, Medibank had arrested a ransomware attack in the staging phase. Stolen data came from the systems of the ahm insurance and the international student insurance services, and comprised customer names, addresses, dates of birth, medicare numbers, policy numbers, and phone numbers. name: Ransomware group 'ALPHV/BlackCat' gained access to confined part of the computer system of the Australian commercial law firm HWL Ebsworth and stole information of 65 government entities in April 2023
description:
The 'ALPHV/BlackCat' ransomware group gained access to a confined part of the computer system of the Australian commercial law firm HWL Ebsworth and stole information of 65 government entities in April 2023, the hacker group claimed in a post on its leak site on 28 April 2023.
In June, the law firm confirmed the cyber incident and said the compromised information included driver's licenses, passports and birth certificate details. ALPHV/BlackCat  leaked some of the stolen information on 9 June 2023.

name: Qilin Ransomware Group suspected of targeting Independent Australian Authority Court Services Victoria (CSV) on 8 December 2023
description:
The Court Services Victoria (CSV) discovered on 21 December 2023 that unidentified threat actors had gained access to the audio-visual in-court technology network and thereby sensitive video recordings, audio recordings and transcription services on 8 December. In response, the CSV isolated and disconnected affected networks.

name: LockBit targeted Austrian municipality of Korneuburg with ransomware on 5 February 2024
description: Initially unknown actors targeted the Austrian municipality of Korneuburg with ransomware on 5 February 2024. Following the incident, the entire IT network was shut down and services had to be switched to manual procedures upon the activation of an emergency plan. The municipality received external assistance from IT specialists to restore the systems. Law enforcement authorities as well as the public prosecutor initiated an investigation. name: The Russian state-sponsored hacking group NoName057(16) disrupted several official websites of Austrian political parties and institutions beginning on 23 September 2024
description:
The Russian state-sponsored hacking group NoName057(16) disrupted several official websites of Austrian political parties and institutions beginning on 23 September 2024, the chief executive officer of the detective agency Foreus, Stefan Embacher, attributed in an article by the television station OE24 on 24 September.
On Monday, 23 September 2024, the websites of the Austrian People's Party (ÖVP) and the Social Democratic Party of Austria (SPÖ) were disrupted. On 24 September 2024, the website of the Communist Party of Austria (KPÖ) was also disrupted. On 25 September 2024, the Ministry of De

name: Pinch duke

name: Kremlin-Linked COLDRIVER aka Star Blizzard Launched Spear-Phishing Campaign Targeting Eastern European Civil Society and US-Based Free Russia Foundation Between April and September 2024
description:
Between April and June 2024, the Kremlin-linked cyberespionage group COLDRIVER aka Star Blizzard conducted a spear-phishing campaign targeting civil society members, NGOs, and the US-based Free Russia Foundation, a pro-democracy think tank. The group focused on organizations operating in Eastern Europe, sending phishing emails containing malicious PDF links designed to compromise victims' systems.
COLDRIVER impersonated trusted contacts to lure targets into downloading the malicious PDFs. Once accessed, victims' devices were scanned, and data was transmitted to command-and-control servers host

name: Russian-aligned threat actor TAG-70 targeted various governmental entities in Europe and Central Asia using Roundcube vulnerability in October 2023
description:
The Russian-aligned threat actor TAG-70 was observed to target more than 80 organisations with a regional focus in Eastern European countries such as Ukraine, Georgia and Poland, Recorded Future disclosed in a February 2024 report. The group, which shares overlaps with activity tracked as Winter Vivern, UAC-0114 and TA473, focused on embassies of various states and other political entities, suggesting espionage-related tasking aligning with Russian and Belarusian interests.
Additional targets included entities in critical infrastructure sectors such as research, science, transport or media. TA

name: Pro-Russian Hacking Group 'NoName057(16)' Suspected Of Disrupting Numerous Belgian Government Websites On 9 November 2023
description: On 9 November, several Belgian government websites were temporarily unavailable, possibly due to yet another cyber-attack, which is believed to be of pro-Russian origin. The hacker group 'NoName057(16)' expressed their dissatisfaction with Belgium's support for Ukraine on their social media channels, suggesting that they could be behind the attack. The group posted a link to a website status monitoring service together with screenshot of what appeared to be timed-out request for loading the website of the Chamber of Representative, Belgium's lower house of parliament. The Centre for Cyber Secu

name: Russian Ransomware Group Medusa Suspected of Compromising Belgian Waste Management Organisation Limburg.Net on 13 December 2023
description:
On 13 December 2023, Limburg.net, the inter-municipal organisation for waste prevention and collection in the province of Limburg and the city of Diest in Belgium, became the subject of an intrusion through which data from over 300,000 Belgian households was compromised. In response to the breach, all systems were immediately shut down to prevent further damage. The organisation's website remained offline for several days. 
Subsequent investigations revealed that threat actors of suspected Russian origin had infiltrated an old data server and copied documents containing public or outdated data

name: Pro-Russian hacktivist group NoName057(16) carried out DDoS attacks against Belgian govermental websites on 20 February 2024
description: In the afternoon of 20 February 2024, the Russian hacktivist group NoName057(16) briefly disrupted several Belgian governmental websites, including the site of Prime Minister Alexander De Croo and the House of Representatives. On Telegram, NoNameo57 linked its activity to Belgium's financial support for the Ukrainian government. The Belgian Cybersecurity Centre confirmed the attack that lasted for two hours, although the websites were not continually down during this timeframe.

name: Russian state-sponsored hacker group NoName057(16) disrupted several government websites in Belgium beginning on 7 October 2024
description:
The Russian state-sponsored hacker group "NoName057(16)" launched a DDoS attack on several government websites and on several infrastructure targets in Belgium between 7 and 8 October 2024, justifying its attack due to Belgium's planned donation of three Caesar artillery units; while NoName declared the attack on its Telegram channel, it was confirmed by the Belgian Cybersecurity Centre (CBC) and the affected institutions.
The attack on municipal websites took place on 7 October and temporarily disrupted the affected servers and impacted the provinces of Liège, Limburg, East Flanders, Flemish
receiver_subcategory: ['Transportation', 'Civil service / administration', 'Government / ministries', 'Advocacy / activists (e.g. human rights organizations)', 'Legislative']

name: Unattributed actors disrupted Bermudian government IT systems beginning 20 September 2023
description:
Unattributed actors disrupted government IT systems of the British Overseas Territory Bermuda beginning 20 September 2023, Bermuda's Home Affairs Minister Walter Roban confirmed in a press conference. Government email and the main switchboard remained non-functioning on 25 September. Government agencies, like the Health Insurance Department, were unable to accept online or process new registrations.
In a press conference on 21 September, Bermuda Premier Edward David Burt declared that the incident also affected another Caribbean country. According to Burt, initial findings indicated the attack

name: Bulgaria presidential election and referendum hack
description: Allegedly Russian hackers disrupted the websites of the bulgarian electoral commission, presidency and other government institutions in the course of the upcoming presidential elections and the referendum on 6th of novermber.
initiator_name: Fancy Bear/APT28/Sofacy/Pawn Storm/Group 74/Sednit/Tsar Team/Forest Blizzard fka STRONTIUM/Grizzly Steppe/SNAKEMACKEREL/IRON TWILIGHT/TG-4127/Group G0007/ITG05/BlueDelta (GRU, 85th Main Special Servic

name: BadRabbit - 2017
description: A threat actor launched a ransomware operation on networks in several countries, mainly in Russia. The operation is believed to have disrupted the Kiev metro system and the Odessa airport. In October 2018, the United Kingdom attributed this incident to Russian military intelligence GRU. But it seems contested, whether it was the work of Fancy Bear or Telebots (aka Sandworm).
initiator_name: Fancy Bear/APT28/Sofacy/Pawn Storm/Group 74/Sednit/Tsar Team/Forest Blizzard fka STRONTIUM/Grizzly Steppe/SNAKEMACKEREL/IRON TWILIGHT/TG-4127/Group G0007/ITG05/BlueDelta (GRU, 85th Main Special Servic

name: The Bulgarian post was disrupted in April 2022
description: The Bulgarian Post was hit by a cyber attack of unknown origin, but Bulgarian cybersecurity experts suspect Russian involvement behind the attack. These hackers utilized Delphi software, of which its users are "99 %" in Russia, and disrupted postal service in order to cause tension.

name: Pro-Russian group KillNet disrupted various Bulgarian websites on 15 October 2022
description: Russian hacktivist group KillNet disrupted various Bulgarian websites, including of government, airports, media and a telecommunication company, on 15 October 2022, according to statements by the hackers

name: First Phase Dragonfly aka EnergeticBear (2011-2014)
description:
The Dragonfly group, which is also known by other vendors as EnergeticBear, appears to have been in operation since at least 2011 and may have been active even longer than that. Dragonfly initially targeted defense and aviation companies in the US and Canada before shifting its focus mainly to US and European energy firms in early 2013. In their campaign against companies and organizations in the international energy sector, including oil and gas firms, nuclear power plants, and utility and power transmission companies. they used the malware "Havex". 
An US indictment from August 26, 2021 char
initiator_name: Marat Valeryevich Tyukov (FSB, Center 16, Military Unit 71330)

name: Tainted Leaks 2016
description: Documents stolen from a prominent journalist and critic of the Russian government were manipulated and then released/leaked. The operation against the journalist led us to the discovery of a larger phishing operation, with over 200 unique targets spanning 39 countries (including members of 28 governments).

name: Wellmess/WellMail - 2020
description: The Russian hacking group APT29 continues to use a malware called WellMess to attack research facilities for COVID-19 vaccines, although in 2020 the malware was already attributed to APT by the U.S., U.K. and Canada.

name: Pro-Russian hacktivists attack several critical infrastructure entities in the beginning of 2024
description: In a recent report, CISA, the CCCS, the NCSC-UK and several other US government agencies disclosed compromises of pro-Russian hacktivists targeting small-scale operational technology (OT) systems in North American and European Water and Wastewater Systems (WWS), Dams, Energy, and Food and Agriculture Sectors. These hacktivists sought to compromise modular, Internet-exposed industrial control systems (ICS) through their software components, such as human machine interfaces (HMIs), by exploiting virtual network computing (VNC) remote access software and default passwords. The malicious activity

name: Pro-Russian Hacking Group Zarya disrupted Canadian gas pipeline on 25 February 2023
description: According to leaked US intelligence documents, Zarya, a suspected spin-off of the pro-Russian hacktivist group Killnet, attacked a Canadian gas pipeline in February 2023.  While the activity did not cause any physical damage, Zarya - in communications with Russia's domestic intelligence service FSB from 15 February - claimed that it could increase valve pressure and trigger emergency shutdowns. Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau appeared to confirm the incident, acknowledging reports about the event and stating “that there was no physical damage to any Canadian energy infrastructure follow

name: Lockbit ransomware group stole data from Canada's electricity grid operator Commission des services electriques de Montreal (CSEM) on 3 August 2023
description:
The Lockbit ransomware group stole data from Canada's electricity grid operator Commission des services electriques de Montreal (CSEM) on 3 August 2023, CSEM reported in a statement via Facebook on 28 August 2023.
On the same day, according to the CSEM announcement, the Lockbit ransomware group also leaked some of the exfiltrated data, adding CSEM to its victim list on 30 August. CSEM noted that this data relates to projects conducted under public procurement processes, with associated documents being openly available.

name: 'NoName057(16)' took down websites of Québec government in DDoS attack on 12-13 September 2023
description:
The websites of eight government websites in Québec, Canada, were taken down by a DDoS attack in the night between 12 and 13 September 2023, which Québec's cybersecurity minister, Éric Caire, and IT security expert Steve Waterhouse, linked to the pro-Russian hacktivist group 'NoName057(16)'.
According to Waterhouse, the attempts at disruption targeted the websites of the Treasury Board Secretariat; the financial regulator Autorité des Marchés Financiers (AMF); the Canadian Securities Administrators, La Société de financement des infrastructures locales du Québec; the provincially-owned investm

name: Monokle
description: Lookout has discovered a highly targeted mobile malware threat that uses a new and sophisticated set of custom Android surveillanceware tools called Monokle that has possible connections to Russian threat actors. Lookout research indicates these tools are part of a targeted set of campaigns and are developed by the St.Petersburg, Russia-based company, Special Technology Centre, Ltd. (STC,Ltd.orSTC).

name: Fancy Bear Summer Campaign
description: In Summer 2019 the Russian APT Fancy Bear attacked various embassies of Eastern European and Central Asian countries.
initiator_name: Fancy Bear/APT28/Sofacy/Pawn Storm/Group 74/Sednit/Tsar Team/Forest Blizzard fka STRONTIUM/Grizzly Steppe/SNAKEMACKEREL/IRON TWILIGHT/TG-4127/Group G0007/ITG05/BlueDelta (GRU, 85th Main Special Servic

name: Lockbit suspected of disrupting IT systems of Industrial and Commercial Bank of China in the United States on 8 November 2023
description:
Lockbit 3.0 ransomware was used to disrupt financial services systems of the Industrial and Commercial Bank of China (ICBC) in the United States on 8 November 2023, the Financial Times reported. Locked systems interfered with the banks ability to settle US Treasury trades for parts of the day.
Whether Lockbit directly conducted the operation or provided tools to a separate actor as part of its ransomware-as-a-service scheme was not immediately reported. ICBC has not been listed on the group's leak site, which may also be a reflection of a common practice among ransomware groups to not identify

name: Russian State-Sponsored Hacking Group TAG-110 Hacked Human Rights Groups, Private Security Companies, And Educational Institutions In Central Asia, East Asia, And Europe Between July 2024 And November
description: Between July 2024 and November 2024, Russian state-sponsored threat actors, including Unit 29155 and TAG-110, likely linked to the Russian cyber-espionage group APT28 (also known as Fancy Bear/APT28/Sofacy/Pawn Storm/Group 74/Sednit/Tsar Team/Forest Blizzard fka STRONTIUM/Grizzly Steppe/SNAKEMACKEREL/IRON TWILIGHT/TG-4127/Group G0007/ITG05/BlueDelta (GRU, 85th Main Special Service Center (GTsSS) Military Unit 26165)), carried out a cyber-espionage campaign targeting human rights groups, private security companies, and educational institutions in Central Asia, East Asia, and Europe. The attacke

name: Russian APTs hack Czech MFAI
description: The Czech Security Intelligence Service (BIS) blamed two cyber-espionage groups--known as Turla and APT28 (Sofacy or Fancy Bear)--for hacks of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MFA), Ministry of Defense, and the Army of the Czech Republic. The hacks took place in different campaigns across 2016 and 2017. The BIS detected several attacks against Czech military targets, officials said."The wave of spearphishing emails targeted mainly people from military diplomacy deployed in Europe. [...]A similar spearphishing attack targeted also European arms companies and a borderguard of a European state.""
initiator_name: Turla/Waterbug/Venomous Bear/Snake/Uroburos/Group 88/Secret Blizzard fka KRYPTON/G0010/UAC-0003 (FSB Centre 16, Unit 71330)

name: Attack on Czech MFA
description: Czech Authorities blame the GRU`s Fancy bear for a DDoS-Attack against the Czech Ministry of Foreign Affairs.

name: Russian cyberspies vs. Slovak government - 2021
description: Between February and July, members of the Slovak government were victims of spear phishing campaigns. The two Slovak security companies ESET and IstroSec attributed the Russian group Dukes/Nobelium/APT29 as the attackers. After these attacks were made public, other campaigns against officials in 13 other European countries were uncovered.

name: The pro-Russian group Killnet disrupted both state and private targets in Czech Republic in April 2022
description:
The pro-Russian hacktivist group Killnet disrupted both state and private targets in the Czech Republic from April 18 to 19, 2022, according to messages on the hacktivist group's own Telegram channel.
The hacktivist group on April 19, 2022, and listed several Czech targets, including the lower house of the Czech Parliament, the railroad company, the Commercial Bank, the telecommunications company O2, and four airports Karlovy-Vary, Pardubice, Ostrava, and Brno-Turany. 
In a press conference after a cabinet meeting, the Czech Interior Minister said on April 20, 2022, that this was an attack aga

name: Pro-Russian hacker group NoName057(16) targeted websites of Czech presidential candidates with DDoS attacks in January 2023
description: In January 2023, the websites of candidates Petr Pavel and Tomáš Zima running for the 2023 Czech presidential elections were targeted with DDoS attacks. Zima's website was first attacked on 11 January and again on 13 January. On the latter date, expanded to Pavel's website. The pro-Russian hacker group NoName057(16) claimed responsibility for the attacks on Telegram. The websites of the non-profit organisation Hlídač státu and the Czech Ministry of Foreign Affairs were also targeted, although the latter unsuccessfully. The group initiated a DDoS collaborator payment program, paying people for
receiver_subcategory: ['Election infrastructure / related systems', 'Advocacy / activists (e.g. human rights organizations)']

name: Website of Czech banks and Prague Stock Exchange hit by DDoS attack from Russia-linked NoName057(16)
description: The National Cyber and Information Security Agency of Czechia (NUKIB) reported that the pro-Russian hacktivist group NoName057(16) launched a DDoS attack against the websites and online banking systems of five Czech banks (Komercni Banka, CSOB, Air Bank, Fio Banka and Ceska sporitelna) and the Prague Stock Exchange on Wednesday, 30 August 2023, disrupting services and temporarily restricting the monetary withdrawals of clients. The network outages did not affect banks' internal systems or client accounts. Card payments and cash withdrawals remained uninterrupted. The temporary inaccessibility

name: Pro-Russian hacking group 'NoName057(16)' suspected of attacking Czech government agencies, Prague airport and national police with DDoS attack on 24 October 2023
description: On 24 October 2023, a series of DDoS attacks disrupted access to various (government) websites across the Czech Republic. The activities, carried out by the pro-Russian hacker group NoName057, as confirmed by the cybersecurity company GenDigital, overwhelmed servers hosting of the Ministry of the Interior, the Czech Police, the Prague Airport and both chambers of parliament. While access to the websites was gradually restored around 2pm the same day, the government portal remained inaccessible for a longer period of time.

name: Fancy Bear vs. Denmark
description: Danish armed forces personnel have had their email shacked from 2015 to 2017, Denmark’s security service said. The hack has been attributed to‘Fancy Bear,’ a hacking group said to have connections to Russia.

name: Unknown threat actors carried out a DDoS cyberattack against presumably multiple Danish municipalities on 9 December 2024
description:
On 9 December 2024, unknown threat actors carried out a DDoS cyberattack against the municipal websites of presumably multiple Danish municipalities. The ones confirming outages included Albertslund, Dragr, Esbjerg, Frederiksberg, Frederikshavn, Greve, Guldborgsund, Haderslev, Hedensted, Horsens, Ikast-Brande, Mariagerfjord, Mors, Nstved, Aabenraa, and Aarhus. The Municipality of Vejle confirmed the DDoS attack on Facebook, causing its website outage.
The pro-Russian hacktivist group NoName057(16) announced DDoS attacks on 9 December 2024, against several Danish municipalities, including Aarhu

name: Russian state-sponsored hacking group NOBELIUM (aka APT29) targeted foreign ministries and diplomatic entities of mostly NATO and EU member states beginning in October 2022
description:
Russian state-sponsored hacking group NOBELIUM (aka APT29) targeted foreign ministries and diplomatic entities of mostly NATO and EU member states beginning on 24 October 2022, according to a technical report of the Polish Military Counterintelligence Service (SVK) Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT Polska).
To a lesser extent, foreign ministries and diplomatic entities in Africa were also targeted. 
This cyber operation differs from previous ones in terms of the new tools used, which include two downloaders SNOWYAMBER and QUARTERRIG as well as the loader HALFRIG. The two downloaders were

name: Russian APT Turla compromised European Ministry of Foreign Affairs and its diplomatic missions in the Middle East by using the LunarWeb and LunarMail backdoors
description: According to a report by ESET, the Russian state-sponsored APT Turla compromised European Ministry of Foreign Affairs and its diplomatic missions in the Middle East by using the LunarWeb and LunarMail backdoors, in a not further specified timeframe, but the compromises happened "more recently" according to the report, though backdoors used in the compromise have been planted going back as far as 2020. ESET further observed varying degrees of sophistication in the compromises. For instance, the careful installation on the compromised server to avoid scanning by security software contrasts with
initiator_name: Turla/Waterbug/Venomous Bear/Snake/Uroburos/Group 88/Secret Blizzard fka KRYPTON/G0010/UAC-0003 (FSB Centre 16, Unit 71330)

name: Turla aka Uroburos aka Snake 2010
description: A cyberespionage campaign involving malware known as Wipbot and Turla has systematically targeted the governments and embassies of a number of former Eastern Bloc countries. It was linked by Gdata to the Russian attack named "agent.btz" on the US in 2008.

description: Threat Actors Use Microsoft OneDrive for Command-and-Control in Attack Campaign

name: Russian state-sponsored hacker group Turla aka Secret Blizzard gained access to Ukrainian and Eastern European defence sectors using the CAPIBAR malware and the KAZUAR backdoor
description:
The Russian state-sponsored hacker group Turla aka Secret Blizzard gained access to Ukrainian and Eastern European defence sectors using the CAPIBAR malware and the KAZUAR backdoor, the Ukrainian CERT disclosed on 18 July 2023 with additional reporting by Microsoft on 19 July.
Turla managed to exfiltrate data from the infected computers, specifically targeting conversations in the desktop app of the messenger Signal.
initiator_name: Turla/Waterbug/Venomous Bear/Snake/Uroburos/Group 88/Secret Blizzard fka KRYPTON/G0010/UAC-0003 (FSB Centre 16, Unit 71330)

name: Estonia 2007
description: Different targets in Estonia attacked on the background of tensions with Russia and Russian minority in Estonia over removal of Soviet war memorial. Estonia accused Russia, but involvement of Russian government is contested and doubted by experts from the IT sector.

name: Pro-Russian hacktivists NoName057(16) targeted the Estonian Central Bank with DDoS attack in June 2022
description: On 7 June 2022, the pro-Russian hacktivists NoName057(16) targeted the Estonian Central Bank with DDoS attacks, according to the IT security company Avast in its report from 6 September 2022.

name: Suspected pro-Russia hacking group attacked Estonian railroad company Elron and its provider Ridango with DDoS attack on 20 September 2023
description:
A suspected pro-Russia hacking group attacked the Estonian railroad company Elron and its provider Ridango with a DDoS attack on 20 September 2023.
Blocking access to the ticketing systems, managed by Ridango, the disruption stopped payments from being processed throughout the morning challening ticket sales online and on trains.

name: Operation Ghost-->The Dukes aka CozyBear aka APT29 - 2019
description: ESET discovered an espionage-campaign conducted by APT 29 against European ministries of foreign affairs from 2013 until at least october 2019. This rejects the hither to existing notion of them being in active since their intervention into the US elections 2016.
initiator_name: Cozy Bear/APT29/Dukes/Group 100/IRON HEMLOCK/Midnight Blizzard fka NOBELIUM/UNC2452/Cozy Duke/YTTRIUM/G0016 (SVR)

name: Open Society Foundation Tainted Leaks
description: Russian-state-sponsored hackers attacked the OSF by creating "TaintedLeaks".

name: World War 3
description: APT28 has recently dispatched several malware distribution campaigns that try to take advantage of a Flash zero-day vulnerability that Adobe patched earlier this week. According to US cyber-security firm Proofpoint, the one which first spotted these attacks, APT28 targeted abroad set of targets across Europe and in the US.

name: Fancy Bear Global Brute Force
description: From 2019 to 2021, Fancy Bear conducted a global brute force campaign targeting the government and private sector.

name: Russian APT Turla breached systems of an unnamed European foreign ministry through phishing campaign
description:
According to ESET, hackers likely within the Russian espionage APT group Turla used a phishing campaign to breach the systems of an unnamed European Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MFA) and three of its diplomatic institutions in the Middle East at an unknown date, though some of the tools observed had been active from 2020 to late 2023.
ESET states that access was likely gained to the systems by using phishing emails including a Word document, which would then install two backdoors ("LunarMail" and "LunarWeb") and a loader ("LunarLoader") into a system in order to establish command-and-control a
initiator_name: Turla/Waterbug/Venomous Bear/Snake/Uroburos/Group 88/Secret Blizzard fka KRYPTON/G0010/UAC-0003 (FSB Centre 16, Unit 71330)

Microsoft dubbed the campaign "BadPilot."

name: Russian state-sponsored hacking group APT29 exploited Windows feature Credential Roaming to gain access to the network of a European diplomatic entity in early 2022
description:
Russian state-sponsored hacking group APT29 exploited the Windows feature Credential Roaming to gain access to the network of a European diplomatic entity in early 2022, according to a technical report by Mandiant. 
The hacking group successfully phished the European diplomatic entity and subsequently used the vulnerability CVE-2022-30170 to elevate its privileges.

name: Russian state-sponsored hacking group APT28 gained access to the networks of various European targets using a zero-day vulnerability in Microsoft Outlook beginning in mid-April 2022
description:
The Russian state-sponsored hacking group APT28 gained access to the networks of at least 15 distinct European targets during mid-April to December 2022 using a zero-day vulnerability in Microsoft Outlook (CVE-2023-23397). The vulnerability was first reported by the Ukrainian CERT and subsequently disclosed in a technical report by Microsoft.
The hackers only had to send an email tailored to the vulnerability to escalate access privileges on the targeted system. Exploitation of the vulnerability is possible without a recipient having to open the actual message. 
The affected European targets a

name: Pro-Russian hackers targeted website of Europe’s air-traffic control agency Eurocontrol with DDoS attack on 19 April 2023
description: Pro-Russian hackers targeted Europe’s air-traffic control agency Eurocontrol with DDoS attacks on19 April 2023. According to the agency, only the website and no air traffic control activities have been affected, due to their isolated networks. However, the more than 2,000 employees had to switch their communication channels as a result of the hack, which affected the Network Manager Operations Center (NMOC) at the Eurocontrol headquarter in Brussels, the Wall Street Journal reported. The pro-Russian hacktivist group Killnet announced potential attacks on Europe's air traffic shortly before the

name: Finnish MFA Hacked by Turla
description: Finnish Foreign Ministry hacked by Turla
initiator_name: Turla/Waterbug/Venomous Bear/Snake/Uroburos/Group 88/Secret Blizzard fka KRYPTON/G0010/UAC-0003 (FSB Centre 16, Unit 71330)

name: Pro-Russian NoName057(16) hacktivist group targeted the Finnish parliament and Sanna Marin's websites in the wake of Finland`s NATO accession in the beginning of April 2023
description: The pro-Russian NoName057(16) hacktivist group targeted the Finnish parliament, at least one other official website and outgoing Finnish PM Sanna Marin's website in the wake of Finland`s NATO accession on 4 April 2023. The hacktivist group stated the following on its Telegram channel: ""Finland will today become the 31st member state of the Nato military alliance. <...> We are sending Finland to Nato, accompanied by denial of service attacks".

name: Pro-Russian Group NoName057(16) carried out DDoS attack against multiple Finnish website on 1 February 2024
description:
The pro-Russian hacktivist group NoName057(16) carried out multiple DDoS attacks against several Finnish websites in early February 2024, as confirmed by the group via its Telegram channel on 2 February 2024. The city administrations of Helsinki, Tampere and Turku were among the entities affected by these DDoS attacks. In addition to the city portals, the Bank of Finland, the Agency for Regulation and Development of Transport and Communications Infrastructure and the Association of Engineers in Finland were also listed as victims.
NoName057(16) linked the selection of targets in Finland to the

name: Russian APT Fancy Bear targeted Emmanuel Macron`s presidential campaign with a hack-and-leak operation between 2017 and 2018
description: Between 2017 and 2018, the Russian state-sponsored APT Fancy Bear leaked Mails of the French Presidential Campaign of Emmanuel Macron with the goal of influencing the French elections.
