Even though the company did not confirm such claims yet, they disclosed that the attack was a ransomware attack, as published on the EquiLend page dedicated to the incident. 
Initial

name: Kansas City Area Transportation Authority hit by Medusa ransomware attack in January 2024
description: As reported on 24 January 2024, the Kansas City Area Transportation Authority (KCATA) was hit by a ransomware attack on 23 January, which led to an interruption in its call service. According to the company, KCATA's public transport services in Missouri and Kansas were not affected. The Medusa ransomware group added the company to its leak list. The criminal collective threatened to release all stolen data, unless KCATA paid a $2 million ransom. KCATA has informed authorities about the incident and launched an investigation into the incident.

name: The BlackSuit ransomware group gained access to the Group Health Cooperative of South Central Wisconsin (GHC-SCW) network and stole personal health information from over 500,000 individuals beginning
description:
The BlackSuit ransomware group gained access to the Group Health Cooperative of South Central Wisconsin (GHC-SCW) network and stole personal health information from over 500,000 individuals beginning at least as far back as 25 January 2024, the affected healthcare provider itself reported in the associated incident notification.
It states that the healthcare provider identified the unauthorised access in the early morning of 25 January 2024 and discovered on 9 February 2024 that the attackers had copied personal health information from the network. This included the names, addresses, telephone

name: The LockBit ransomware group caused IT outage in US county of Fulton on 28 January 2024
description:
On 28 January 2024, Fulton County in the US state of Georgia experienced an IT outage due to a cyber attack by the Lockbit Ransomware Group. The disruptions affected the phone systems and most offices are unable to accept phone calls. of several systems were impaired, including those used for property tax transactions and by the justice system to issue documents, such as marriage licences. The downtown Atlanta office of the Fulton County Tax Commissioner remained closed.
On 13 February 2024, the LockBit ransomware group asserted responsibility for the attack on Fulton County. They provided 25

name: Rhysida ransomware suspected of causing disruption at US Lurie Children's Hospital in Chicago on 1 February 2024
description: The Lurie Children’s Hospital in Chicago, in the US state of Illinois, experienced network disruptions on 1 February 2024. Outages affecting Internet connectivity, email, telephone services, and a virtual consultation platform caused delays in medical services. In response to the incident, certain elective surgeries and procedures had to be postponed. Lurie Children’s Hospital is the primary provider of paediatric healthcare in Illinois, with a total capacity of 360 beds. The network had been offline for at least two weeks, affecting some surgeries that had to rely on manual procedures, as ele

name: ALPHV ransomware group targeted US insurance company Prudential in data breach on 4 February 2024
description: The threat actors gained access to data of the US insurance company Prudential on 4 February 2024. The data breach was detected one day later and is assessed to have affected administrative and user data from certain information technology systems, as well as a small percentage of company user accounts. In an SEC filing from 12 February, which disclosed the incident, Prudential reported that the breach did not materially affect operations and did not otherwise materially impact the company's financial condition. On 16 Feburary, ALPHV ransomware group claimed to be responsible for the data brea

name: BlackCat/ALPHV compromised US Healthcare Provider Change Healthcare Leading to Nationwide Outage of Prescription Processing Services on 21 February 2024
description: On 21 February 2024, UnitedHealth Group revealed that its subsidiary, Change Healthcare, operated by the IT company Optum Solutions, suffered a compromise of its systems. This incident caused a widespread disruption in processing services throughout the United States, leading to challenges for pharmacies in delivering medications to patients. The American Hospital Association, based on open-source statements and press reports, points to the exploitation of a zero-day vulnerability in ScreenConnect, a remote access solution offered by the company ConnectWise, as a contributing factor to the int

name: 3AM Ransomware Group Accessed and Exfiltrated Data from Idaho's Kootenai Health on 22 February 2024
description:
On 22 February 2024, Idaho-based healthcare provider Kootenai Health was targeted by the 3AM ransomware group, resulting in a significant data breach. Hackers gained unauthorized access to Kootenai Health's network and exfiltrated sensitive information belonging to 464,088 individuals, including patients, employees, and their dependents.
The compromised data includes names, dates of birth, Social Security numbers, driver’s licence numbers, medical records, treatment information, and health insurance details. Despite the breach, Kootenai Health's operations continued uninterrupted. Impacted ind

name: Lockbit ransomware group compromised employee account of US pharmaceutical company Crinetics Pharmaceuticals potentially stealing data in March 2024
description: Crinetics Pharmaceuticals, a Nasdaq-listed pharmaceutical development company based in San Diego, California, is investigating an incident after the LockBit ransomware gang claimed to have stolen data from the company. The company discovered suspicious activity in an employee's account, immediately disabled it, initiated a cyber incident response procedure, brought in outside forensic experts and also notified law enforcement. While the company appeared on the LockBit leak site together with a $4 million ransom demand set to expire on 23 March, Crinetics at the time of reporting had not confir

name: LockBit Ransomware Gang Leaked D.C. Department of Insurance, Securities and Banking Data After Gaining Access to Texas-Based Cloud Provider Tyler Technologies' Systems in Late March 2024
description: The LockBit ransomware gang claimed it attacked the Washington, D.C. Department of Insurance, Securities and Banking (DISB) and allegedly stole 800GB of data on 13 April 2024. However, DISB clarified that the data breach originated from a third-party technology provider, Texas-based Tyler Technologies. LockBit threatened to leak 1GB of data to escalate ransom negotiations. DISB redirected inquiries to a statement and declined further comment. Tyler Technologies reported unauthorized access to their cloud infrastructure storing DISB's data and initiated an investigation. They confirmed data lea

name: The Medusa ransomware group gained access to the Paducah Dermatology Clinic in Kentucky and stole a variety of information in the last days of March
description:
The Medusa ransomware group gained access to the Paducah Dermatology Clinic in Kentucky and stole a variety of information in the last days of March, the ransomware group published on its website in early April.
The independent organization SuspectFile investigated this cyber incident and found various stolen information that affected employees patients, including medical histories. SuspectFile wrote that the hackers probably gained access via Remote Desktop Control (RDP) due to weak credentials or stolen credentials and demanded $100,000 worth of Bitcoins by 13 April 2024 if the stolen inform

name: Individual hacker claimed responsibility for alleged data breach of US financial service provider Paysign on 6 March 2024
description: On 6 March 2024, a hacker operating under the pseudonym 'emo' claimed to have breached US financial Service provider Paysign and stolen data 1.2 million customer records containing names, addresses, dates of birth, phone numbers and account balances. Paysign announced it was investigating the claim while stating that no disruption to services had been observed.

name: Medusa ransomware group targeted administration of Henry County in Illinois on 18 March 2024
description:
According to a media report by The Record citing the director of the local Emergency Management Office, the administration of Henry County in the US state of Illinois fell victim to a ransomware attack on 18 March 2024. The incident response team disconnected multiple impacted county government systems to contain the intrusion. 
The ransomware group Medusa claimed responsibility for the attack on its leak site and requested a ransom of $500,000.

name: Nation-state backed attackers exploit zero-day in Palo Alto's firewall against unknown targets since 26 March 2024
description: Nation-state backed attackers have exploited a severe zero-day vulnerability in Palo Alto's firewall against unknown targets since 26 March 2024, according to researchers from Volexity. The CVE-2024-3400 vulnerability was designated with the maximum severity rating of 10.0 and was under active exploitation for at least two weeks. It enables attackers without authentication to execute malicious code with root privileges. Upon exploiting the flaw, the threat actor was observed creating a cronjob that would run every minute to access commands hosted on an external server that would execute via ba

name: Medusa Ransomware Group stole data from East Baton Rouge Sheriff's Office in Louisiana on 29 March 2024
description:
According to local news reporting, the Medusa ransomware group stole data from the East Baton Rouge Sheriff's Office in the US state of Louisiana on 29 March 2024. The obtained data included screenshots of file folders and images of video files, investigators confirmed. 
The Sheriff's Office detected the intrusion and implemented security measures to curtail the threat actor's access.
Medusa published the stolen files on its leak site, including alleged payroll records, staging plans for policy operations and screenshots of CCTV footage. According to an investigator, Medusa demanded a $300,000

name: Medusa ransomware group stole 85GB of data and disrupted systems of the Solano County Library (California, USA) on 5 April 2024
description:
The Medusa ransomware gang claimed the Solano County Library in California as one of its victims in a ransomware attack in which 85GB of data were stolen on 5 April 2024. 
The attack also disrupted the services of the library; WiFi, computer services, phone lines, and internal records-keeping services were all rendered inaccessible by the attack, with the computers being down for over a week. As of 15 April 2024, they were still down. 
The group demanded a $100,000 payment for them to not release the data stolen during the attack, which is believed to include personal data of patrons of the li

name: BlackSuit Ransomware Group gained access to US software company Young Consulting Data between 10–13 April 2024
description:
BlackSuit Ransomware Group gained access to Young Consulting Data between 10–13 April 2024. 
Young Consulting, a software company that develops integrated software solutions for the underwriting, marketing and administering of medical stop-loss insurance, experienced technical difficulties within its computer environment on 13 April 2024. Further investigation determined that an unauthorized actor gained access to Young Consulting's network between 10 April and 13 April 2024 and downloaded copies of certain files. The information involved in the breach varied by individual but included names,

name: The RansomHub group gained access to the computer system of US telecommunication company Frontier Communications beginning at least on 14 April 2024
description: A cybercrime group gained access to the computer system of US telecommunication company Frontier Communications beginning at least on 14 April 2024, the company reported on 15 April 2024. In the beginning of June, the RansomHub group claimed that they have stolen the information of over 2 million customers (5GB). The stolen data includes names, email addresses, SSNs, credits, scores, dates of birth, and phone numbers.

name: The Medusa ransomware group gained access to the servers of Northeast Ohio Neighborhood Health and stole 51GB of data on 15 April 2024
description:
The Medusa ransomware group has claimed to have successfully attacked the servers of Northeast Ohio Neighborhood Health (NEON) on 15 April 2024. During the attack, 51GB of data were exfiltrated and the systems of NEON were locked, with a ransom of $250,000 being demanded. 
NEON is associated with Change Healthcare, which has already been the target of several cyberattacks.

name: Ransomware group Blacksuit steals data from US branch of Swiss pharmaceutical company, Octapharma, on 17 April 2024
description:
Octapharma Plasma, the US branch of Octapharma, a Swiss pharmaceutical company involved in "protein manufacturing," or the manufacturing of plasma-dependent medical therapies, was hit by a ransomware attack by Blacksuit ransomware group on 17 April 2024. 
According to Octapharma's press release on 19 April, the company detected "suspicious activity" on its networks, which resulted in the company having to take its systems offline while an investigation was initiated; this impacted operations of over 100 plasma donation centres in the US. Days later, on 24 April 2024, the Blacksuit ransomware g

name: Akira Ransomware Group Attacked the US Louisiana Special School District with Ransomware in May 2024
description: In June 2024, the Louisiana Special School District fell victim to a ransomware attack by the group Akira, resulting in a data breach that exposed personal and limited health information of students and staff, including national insurance numbers and addresses. The intrusion was discovered on 24 May and affected multiple departments, including the Office of Human Resources and the Louisiana School for the Deaf. Upon discovery, the district notified the Louisiana State Police and remediated the network security vulnerabilities. Affected individuals were notified beginning on 8 July, and the dis

name: Black Basta Ransomware Group Claims To Have Stolen 730 GB Of Data From American Oil Distributor 'Atlas Oil' In May 2024
description:
The Black Basta ransomware group claimed to have stolen 730GB of data from Atlas Oil, one of the largest distributors of fuel in the United States, on 20 May 2024.
According to samples of the stolen data that were leaked as proof of the data theft, the data could include corporate data, payroll information, employee and user IDs, and more. As of 22 May 2024, Atlas Oil had not confirmed the theft of data.

name: The LockBit ransomware group stole data and personal information of some customers from Evolve Bank & Trust
description:
The LockBit ransomware group stole data and personal information of some customers from Evolve Bank & Trust, the technology security company HackManac reported on social media on 23 June 2024.
The LockBit ransomware group first claimed to have hacked and stolen information from the Federal Reserve on 23 June 2024. This proved to be false when HackManac made the connection to Evolve Bank & Trust, and the bank admitted the incident the following day without referring to LockBit, but only to a cybercriminal organisation.
The stolen data included data and personal information of some Evolve retail

name: LockBit allegedly compromised Hawaiian Malama I Ke Ola Health Center with ransomware since mid-May 2024
description:
The LockBit ransomware group has allegedly compromised the systems of Malama I Ke Ola Health Center in Hawaii with ransomware since mid-May 2024. The attack disrupted normal service for two weeks. 
According to the Indian cybersecurity company FalconFeeds, LockBit claimed responsibility for the attack in its dark web forum.
The health center has not confirmed the ransomware attack officially yet.
On 26 September 2024, the Hawaiian Malama I Ke Ola Health Center filed a report with the Maine Attorney General's Office and published a statement on its website. It stated that an investigation revea

name: LockBit Group targeted Systems of the City of Wichita, Kansas and Deploys Encryption Malware on 5 May 2024
description:
On 5 May 2024, an unknown threat actor used encryption malware to lock selected systems of the US City of Wichita, Kansas, resulting in the shutdown of certain computer networks and systems. 
On 7 May 2024, the ransomware group Lockbit claimed responsibility for the attack and added the city of Wichita to its extortion portal noting that it had exfiltrated information from compromised systems. LockBit threatened to release the stolen data if the demanded ransom was not paid by 15 May 2024.

name: RansomHub Ransomware Group Claims To Have Hit The US-American Pharmarcy Chain Rite Aid With Ransomware Beginning In June 2024
description: In June 2024, Rite Aid, the third-largest drugstore chain in the United States, experienced a cyberattack by the RansomHub ransomware group, resulting in a significant data breach. This attack compromised over 10 GB of customer information, affecting around 45 million lines of personal data, including names, addresses, driving licence ID numbers, dates of birth, and Rite Aid rewards numbers. RansomHub claimed responsibility for the attack and posted details on BreachForums, including a screenshot of some of the allegedly stolen data as proof. Rite Aid, which employs over 45,000 staff across mo

name: Unknown threat actor attacked US manufacturer Crown Equipment on 8 June 2024
description:
An unknown threat actor attacked US manufacturer Crown Equipment on 8 June 2024, the firm confirmed in an email to their employers on 20 June 2024. The email details that Crown Equipment's IT system was hacked by an "international cybercriminal organisation," which required the manufacturer to shut down its operating systems. 
The attack caused disruption at the manufacturing plants of the company and employees have been unable to clock in their hours, access service manuals, and deliver machinery in some cases, according to Bleeping Computer reporting.

name: DragonForce Group targeted servers managing 911 services in Southern Californiawith ransomware on 16 July 2024
description: According to the Californian South Bay Regional Public Communications Authority (SBRPCA), the DragonForce Group targeted servers used to manage 911 services in South California with ransomware on 16 July 2024. Among the affected cities are Manhattan Beach, Culver City, Hermosa Beach as well as El Segundo, Hawthorne and Gardena.

name: BlackSuit Ransomware group hit US SaaS provider CDK Global starting 18 June 2024
description: CDK Global, a Software-as-a-Service provider for car and trucker dealers in North America, was hit by a ransomware attack on 18 June 2024 forcing it to shut down its dealer management system. The outage of the system affected roughly 15,000 car dealers in North America, who had to move back to pen and paper to continue business operations. CDK Global is reported to be negotiating with the threat actor to pay the ransom, allegedly to be in the double-digit million dollar figure.  News reporting on 22 June 2024 claimed that BlackSuit Ransomware was behind the incident.

name: BlackSuit breached the network of the Monroe County government on 1 July 2024
description: According to county officials of Monroe, they experienced an intrusion into their networks on 1 July 2024 and that the perpetrator was identified as the ransomware gang BlackSuit. The attack is still under investigation, but the extent of affected data is still unclear.

name: Hacktivist group SiegedSec breached US think tank Heritage Foundation on 2 July 2024
description: On 2 July 2024, the hacktivist group SiegedSec breached the Heritage Foundation which is a prominent conservative think tank based in Washington, D.C. On 9 July 2024, the hacker group published roughly 2 GB of the 200 GB stolen data including the full names, email addresses, passwords, and usernames of people associating with Heritage including users with U.S. government email addresses, Heritage Foundation blogs, material related to The Daily Signal. According to the hackers, they released the data in response to Heritage Foundation’s Project 2025 and to provide “transparency to the public re

name: An unknown threat actor gained unauthorized access to an Azure Sharepoint instance of Fortinet on September 2024
description: Cybersecurity giant Fortinet has confirmed it suffered a data breach after a threat actor with the moniker ‘Fortibitch’ claimed to steal 440GB of files from the company's Microsoft Sharepoint server. Fortinet is one of the largest cybersecurity companies in the world, selling secure networking products like firewalls, routers, and VPN devices. The threat actor claims to have tried to extort Fortinet into paying a ransom, likely to prevent the publishing of data, but the company refused to pay. Fortinet confirmed that customer data was stolen from a "third-party cloud-based shared file drive."

name: The INC RANSOM ransomware gang encrypted and stole data from from US-Based Non-Profit Healthcare Provider McLaren Health Care starting on 6 August 2024
description: ​On 6 August 2024, IT and phone systems at McLaren Health Care hospitals were disrupted following an attack linked to the INC Ransom ransomware operation. McLaren hinted the hospitals had lost access to patient information databases when advising patients to bring detailed information about their current medications to appointments, including physician orders and printed results of recent lab tests. Employees at McLaren Bay Region Hospital in Bay City have shared a ransom note warning that the hospital's systems have been encrypted and stolen data will be published on INC RANSOM ransomware gan

name: Ransomware gang Hunters International breached Omni Family Health in the United States, stealing sensitive information in August 2024
description: The Hunters International ransomware gang compromised Omni Family Health, a nonprofit organization providing healthcare services to communities in California. The group obtained personal information of nearly 470,000 current and former patients and employees in the breach. Omni Family Health discovered the intrusion on 7 August 2024, following claims that information was taken from its systems and leaked on the dark web. The data breach may have exposed varying personal information, including names, addresses, Social Security numbers, dates of birth, health insurance details, and medical infor

name: BlackSuit Ransomware group gained access to systems of the City of Killeen, Texas, on 7 August 2024
description:
According to the City of Killeen, (Texas, USA) the BlackSuit ransomware group gained access to the city's systems on Wednesday, 7 August 2024. 
The city says most of the affected systems are internal, but citizens may experience a delay in essential services at the Utility Collections division.
Upon discovery of the breach, the city took steps to secure their systems to prevent further access. As a precautionary measure, Killeen disconnected from the Bell County Network to contain the spread of the attack.
As of now, the city does not believe that personal or financial information of citizens

name: Qilin Ransomware group claims attack on CODAC Behavioral Healthcare (USA) on 28 August 2024
description:
The Qilin group listed CODAC Behavioral Healthcare, a non-profit health care treatment organization, as one of their latest victims on 28 August 2024. Within the stolen data are financial information, pictures of ID cards, a list of staff members—including their Social Security Numbers—and healthcare cards. 
This attack is very similar to the one on pathology lab services provider Synnovis in June 2024.

name: Rhysida ransomware group target Port of Seattle and Seattle-Tacoma International Airport (USA) with Ransomware on 24 August 2024
description:
Rhysida ransomware group targeted the Port of Seattle, which also operates the Seattle-Tacoma (Sea-Tac) International Airport, with a cyberattack on 24 August 2024. The outages began early Saturday and took down several systems. While the full scale of the outage has not been made public, the Port of Seattle websites, email and phone systems were also affected. 
Security operations at Sea-Tac Airport were not impacted with 72,000 people flying out on Sunday, although the baggage sorting systems and some terminal screens at Sea-Tac Airport were disrupted. Travellers have been advised to allow e

name: RansomHub stole data from the nonprofit organization Planned Parenthood in late August 2024
description: In late August, Planned Parenthood suffered a cyberattack causing a disruption and sensitive data may be stolen. Planned Parenthood is a non-profit organization that provides sexual health care services. It is not yet known whether any personal information about patients might have been stolen, but that could potentially be devastating. The group behind the attack, Ransomhub, has claimed responsibility on their leak site. The listing on the leak site shows financial information, court papers, and insurance certificates.

name: Ransomware gang Nitrogen gained access to network of SRP Federal Credit Union USA, beginning on September 5, 2024
description: Between September 5, 2024, and November 4, 2024, Unknown Threat Actors, later identified as Nitrogen ransomware gang, breached the confidentiality of SRP Federal Credit Union systems, potentially acquiring files containing sensitive information, including names, Social Security numbers, drivers license numbers, dates of birth, and financial information, impacting approximately 240,000 individuals. SRP Federal Credit Union, detected the incident and law enforcement was notified, leading to an investigation that concluded on November 22, 2024. Nitrogen ransomware gang claimed responsibility for

name: The BianLian Ransomware Group Gained Access to Boston Children's Health Physicians (BCHP) and Stole Certain Files Beginning at Least on 10 September 2024
description:
The BianLian ransomware group claimed on 16 October 2024, that it gained access to Boston Children's Health Physicians (BCHP) and stole certain files starting at least on 10 September 2024.
The BCHP incident notification stated that an unnamed BCHP IT provider detected suspicious activity on its network and subsequently informed the BCHP on 6 September 2024. On 10 September 2024, the BCHP also discovered unauthorized activity in limited portions of the network. Working with an IT security firm, the unauthorized access on 10 September 2024, was confirmed, as was the extraction of certain files.

name: RansomHub ransomware gang hacked HCF Management in the United States between 17 September 2024 and 3 October 2024
description: On 17 September 2024, the ransomware gang RansomHub gained unauthorized access to HCF Management's computer systems. The breach affected nearly 70,000 people. Heritage Health Care reported the largest breach, affecting 12,162 people. Hempfield Manor was the single facility with the most patients reported affected, at 4,744. RansomHub added HCF to its dark web site on 29 October 2024, and claimed to have published 250 Gbytes of HCF's data. HCF learned of the breach on 3 October 2024, and engaged an external computer forensic firm to assist. The investigation found that threat actors accessed an

name: Interlock ransomware stole sensitive data of 1.4 million patients from Texas Tech University Health Sciences Center in the USA beginning on September 17, 2024
description: Interlock ransomware group, breached the Texas Tech University Health Sciences Center and its El Paso counterpart in September 2024, potentially exposing the data of 1.4 million patients, including full name, date of birth, physical address, Social Security number, driver's license number, government ID number, financial account information, health insurance information, medical information, billing/claims data, diagnosis and treatment information, by disrupting computer systems and applications, and stealing sensitive data between September 17 and September 29, 2024, with the incident being r

name: RansomHub ransomware gang stole personal and health information of US BayMark Health Services between September and October 2024
description: Unknown Threat Actors breached the IT systems of BayMark Health Services, the largest US addiction treatment provider, between September 24, 2024, and October 14, 2024, stealing personal and health information of an undisclosed number of patients, including names, Social Security numbers, driver's license numbers, dates of birth, services received, insurance information, and treatment and diagnostic information. The breach was discovered on October 11, 2024, following an IT systems disruption, and was claimed by the RansomHub ransomware gang, which uploaded 1.5TB of stolen files on its dark we

name: Stormous Ransomware Group Stole Sensitive Data from Crypto Payment Service Transak in October 2024
description: In October 2024, Stormous ransomware group compromised the Transak company, which provides an 'onamp' service for many famous blockchain companies, by exploiting a data leak caused by employees using laptops for non-work purposes, affecting some 93,000 unique users, including passports, ID cards, and personal photos, and is now trying to negotiate with the company, demanding $30,000 to remove the stolen data, which includes classified private documents, government identifications, addresses, financial statements, and personal photos of users, with the company classifying the damage as 'low or

name: IntelBroker, Together with Hacking Groups EnergyWeaponUser and zjj, Breached the Systems of Cisco on 6 October 2024 and Exfiltrated Customer Data
description: Unknown threat actors, allegedly IntelBroker, EnergyWeaponUser and zjj, breached the systems of Cisco, a networking hardware and software manufacturer, on 6 October 2024, and exfiltrated data belonging to it and its customers, including Github projects, Gitlab Projects, SonarQube projects, source code, hard-coded credentials, certificates, customer SRCs, Cisco confidential documents, Jira tickets, API tokens, AWS Private buckets, Cisco Technology SRCs, Docker Builds, Azure Storage buckets, Private & Public keys, SSL Certificates, and Cisco Premium Products, as well as the production source cod

name: RansomHub Gang Breached The US-City Of Coppell, Texas On 23 October 2024
description: On 23 October 2024, the RansomHub gang breached the confidentiality and availability of the information systems of the City of Coppell, Texas with a ransomware attack. The City of Coppell reported widespread technology issues, including an internet outage, unavailable WiFi at city facilities, and disruptions to library services, permitting and inspection platforms, and the municipal court. The City of Coppell announced the attack on 23 October 2024 and by the RansomHub gang on 25 November 2024. The City Manager of the City of Coppell, Mike Land, informed the public about the attack and its pot

name: The hacker known as abyss0 allegedly stole a large amount of presumably customer data from financial technology company Finastra
description: On 7 November 2024, Finastra detected suspicious activity on its internally hosted file transfer platform, which led to the discovery of a large-scale data breach. Using the nickname 'abyss0', an unknown threat actor had stolen over 400 gigabytes of data, including customer information, and began selling it on the cybercrime community BreachForums on 8 November 2024. This actor also tried to sell the stolen data before, on 31 October 2024. Therefore, the breach is believed to have occurred at least a week before detection, with the threat actor gaining access to Finastra's file-sharing system

name: Embargo Ransomware Gang launched a ransomware attack on Memorial Hospital and Manor in the United States on 2 November 2024
description:
Embargo Ransomware Gang launched a ransomware attack on Memorial Hospital and Manor, an 80-bed hospital in Bainbridge, Georgia, on 2 November 2024, disrupting the hospital's Electronic Health Records (EHR) system, which was discovered early Saturday morning when employees saw notifications of potential risks found by the virus protection software, and the hospital has since reverted to paper-based processes while evaluating options to restore and recover its EHR, as disclosed by the hospital in a statement on Facebook on 3 November 2024.
The Embargo ransomware gang claimed responsibility for t

name: Intel Broker leaked personal data of 290,762 individuals from MIT Technology Review Magazine in the United States via a third-party contractor on 4 November 2024
description: The hacker known as Intel Broker breached MIT Technology Review Magazine via a third-party contractor, leaking nearly 300,000 user records on Breach Forums on 4 November 2024. The data includes full names, email addresses, and activity details, posing risks for phishing and targeted scams. Intel Broker, notorious for recent attacks on high-profile organizations, alleges that the data includes Personally Identifiable Information. Hackread.com analyzed the leaked data and can confirm it includes personal details, which, while not as sensitive as financial data, could be leveraged in phishing att

name: The Termite ransomware group disrupted the managed services hosted environment of supply chain management and software provider Blue Yonder since at least 21 November 2024
description:
The Termite ransomware group disrupted the managed services hosted environment of supply chain management and software provider Blue Yonder since at least 21 November 2024, Blue Yonder reported in an incident notification for the first time on 22 November 2024.
The incident involved a ransomware attack, and at least at the coffee chain Starbucks, the incident disrupted the system they use to track and manage their employees' schedules. The retailer Wm Morrison Supermarkets was also affected. In December 2024, the Termite ransomware group claimed to have exfiltrated 680 GB of data, including da

name: Black Basta Ransomware Gang Breached Mortgage Investors Group in the USA Using Ransomware Between 11 December 2024 and 12 December 2024
description: Mortgage Investors Group (MIG), a Tennessee-based mortgage lender, was breached by the Black Basta Ransomware Gang in a cyberattack that started on 11 December 2024 and was discovered on 12 December 2024, resulting in the exposure of sensitive personal information, including full names and financial information, of an undisclosed number of customers. The company hired a vendor to identify affected individuals and notify them directly in several weeks.

name: IntelBroker Stole Documents from US-American Information Technology Company Hewlett Packard Enterprise Using API, WePay, and GitHub Repositories Breach on 16 January 2025
description: On 16 January 2025, the US-American multinational information technology company Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) was informed by IntelBroker that they had stolen documents from HPE's developer environments, including source code, certificates, and personal information. IntelBroker claimed to have had access to HPE's API, WePay, and GitHub repositories for at least two days. HPE immediately launched an investigation and activated their cyber response protocols. As of 20 January 2025, HPE had not found any evidence of a security breach. The incident was reported by BleepingComputer, who was inf

name: Medusa ransomware group breached SimonMed Imaging in the US, stealing sensitive data and exposing private information of over 132,000 individuals, including patients and employees, by using ransomware
description: On 8 February 2025, Medusa Ransomware Group claimed to have breached SimonMed Imaging, a US-based diagnostic imaging company, stealing over 212 GB of sensitive data and exposing private information of more than 132,000 individuals, including patients and employees, before demanding a $1 million ransom in Bitcoin. The group posted 45 proof files online. A company representative said the breach occurred last week, but SimonMed “interrupted” hackers and “no data was encrypted.” Clinical systems were not impacted by this “unauthorized activity". Furthermore, "to immediately contain the situation,

name: Unknown threat actors hacked  crypto-focused Infini Neobank on 24 February 2025 stealing USD Coins worth $49.5 million
description: On 24 February 2025, Infini Neobank was hacked, resulting in the theft of $49.5 million USDC, which was then transferred to the Ethereum network. The attacker traded the stolen USDC for Ethereum-based DAI and purchased 17.696 ETH, later transferring it to the address 0xfcc8...6e49. The attack was likely carried out by obtaining a private key, with the private key to the address 0xc49b...e3e1 possibly being leaked. The stolen assets were transferred to wallets associated with TornadoCash, an anonymization protocol. An Infini developer, who secretly retained executive authority after the project

name: Unknown threat actor dubbed Codefinger encrypt S3 buckets in Amazon Web Services using ServerSide Encryption with Customer Provided Keys at least until January 2025
description: On 13 January 2025, an ongoing ransomware campaign was disclosed by Halcyon, where the group/actor Codefinger encrypted at least two Amazon S3 buckets using AWS's ServerSide Encryption with Customer Provided Keys, demanding ransoms to receive the decryption key. Codefinger used compromised AWS credentials to locate victims' keys, generated an encryption key locally to encrypt the targets' data, and set a seven-day file deletion policy using the S3 Object Lifecycle Management API. Ransom notes were dropped on all affected directories, instructing the victim to pay ransom on a given Bitcoin addr

name: "Operation Parliament"
description: An unknown hacker group with the capabilities of a state-sponsored APT attacked several countries with espionage malware. Most of the targets were located in the MENA-region.

name: Pro-Trump retaliation: Liker.com leak
description: Hacktivists hacked the anti-Trump social Network Liker.com and around 400 records are leaked.

name: The TA558 cybercrime group stole data from over 300 computer systems in various countries and sectors since at least 2018
description:
The TA558 cybercrime group employed steganography in over 300 attacks on various countries across multiple sectors since at least 2018. 
The information about TA558 comes from research by the Russian cyber security provider Positive Technologies. According to their findings, TA558 concentrates its attacks on Latin America, but European and Asian countries are also among the victims. Many of the attacks were carried out using malicious code embedded in images and RTF documents, so-called steganography attacks.
This cyber incident was already addressed last year by some companies and individuals

name: ExPresidents breached the National Party website in Uruguay using a Cross-Site-Scripting technique on 23-24 September 2024
description: The hacktivist group Ex-Presidents breached the National Party website in Uruguay on 23 September 2024, leaving a public threat, after having already violated the system in August 2024 with a similar message, including a specific date, '13/08/2024', anticipating potential actions. The attack was carried out using a Cross-Site-Scripting technique, according to Birmingham Cyber Arms, and was reported by El Observador. The National Party has responded by taking preventive measures to mitigate future incidents and is actively working to strengthen their cyber security measures.

name: Phishing campaign against EMEA and APAC governments
description: In a large-scale campaign, various government departments in APAC and EMEA countries, such as Ukraine, Turkey, Russia or Pakistan, became victims of phishing. IT company Cyjax sees similarities in the campaign to an operation against Ukraine at the beginning of the COVID-19 pandemic, attributed to the groups UNC1151 and Hades.

name: Machete vs. Venezuelan Army
description: A cyber-espionage group known as "Machete" has been observed stealing sensitive files from the Venezuelan military,according to an ESET report published today.

name: Hack Argentino team vs. Venezuela Government
description: A hacker with twitter handle "Libero america Mu" from HackArgentinoteam, has gained access to multiple Venezuela Government websites and defaced them, leaving anti-government slogans.

name: Falling Dominos
description: Several Hacker Groups defaced and hacked websites of venezuelan Gov. and military Websites to support opposition during protests

name: Medusa Ransomware Group targeted Venezuelan Telecommunications Company Digitel With Ransomware on 2 February 2024
description: The ransomware group Medusa initiated a ransomware attack against the Venezuelan telecommunications company Digitel on 2 February 2024. The criminal collective compromised personal user data and encrypted corporate databases. To unlock the data and refrain from leaking internal information, the cybercriminals demanded a payment of $5 million within nine days by 11 February. On 12 February, personal data of millions of users have been released.

name: Venezuelan National Electoral Council allegedly targeted with DDoS after the presidential election on 28 July 2024
description:
According to President Maduro, the Venezuelan National Electoral Council was allegedly targeted with DDoS after the Presidential election on 28 July 2024, which he stated as the reason for why the authorities have not released detailed vote counts at that time. Furthermore, he blamed Elon Musk for the attack and argued that the election system has been hacked as part of a plot against him. However, the hacking collective Anonymous also claimed the hack for itself.
There have been no external verifications of the DDoS operation, but the Venezuelan state airline Conviasa was also disrupted a few

name: Anonymous targeted the website of the Venezuelan airline Conviasa and other government websites in August 2024
description:
The hacktivist collective Anonymous targeted the website of the Venezuelan state airline Conviasa on 2 August, 2024. Transport Minister Ramón Velásquez made the allegation, also associating the collective with the United States. In the past, the US government imposed sanctions against the airline in order to limit its air operations. The action is part of a broader campaign against the country after the election of president Maduro on 28 July faced intense fraud allegations.
On 13 August 2024, El Mundo reported on Anonymous' campaign ‘Long live free Venezuela’ in general. This campaign is said

name: Kaputskiy vs. Venezuelan Army
description: Kaputskiy hacks Venezuelan Army and exposes details of  3,000 accounts

name: SpringDragon aka LotusBlossom
description: Since as early as 2012, the maintargets of SpringDragon attacks are high profile governmental organizations and political parties, education institutions such as universities, as well as companies from the telecommunications sector.

name: Anonymous vs. Vietnam Government
description: Anonymous Hacks Vietnam Govt websites Against Human Rights Abuse

name: Watering Hole Attacks - OceanLotus
description: Researchers from ESET discover a new watering hole campaign targeting 21 distinct websites in Southeast Asia carried out by OceanLotus.
initiator_name: APT32/Ocean Lotus/Sea Lotus/Canvas Cyclone fka BISMUTH

name: ChamelGang
description: The previously unknown APT ChamelGang targeted institutions, such as the government, aviation and energy sectors, of a total of ten countries in two attacks. The group disguised its malware and network infrastructure as legitimate domains, such as McAffee, Microsoft, or TrendMicro.

name: Dark Pink APT targeted state and corporate organizations in the Asia-Pacific and Europe since mid-2021
description:
In an eponymous cyber-operation, a newly-discovered APT group named Dark Pink was observed by Group-IB Global to have launched a series of successful malware and spearphishing campaigns against government, military, and corporate entities predominantly based in the Asia-Pacific (APAC) region. Initial attacks date back to at least June 2022, with some indications that the group may have been active as early as May 2021. 
The group likely operates out of the APAC region. Group-IB analysis acknowledges earlier reporting by Chinese cybersecurity researchers at the Anheng Information Shadows Huntin

name: Anonymous vs. Yemen Ministry of Information
description: Anonymous takes down the websites of Yemen’s Ministry of Information, as well as Yemeni President Ali Abdullah Saleh

name: Operation Slingshot
description: Kaspersky revealed an allegedly US-counter terrorism cybercampaign in MENA countries, especially Kenya and Yemen.

name: Operation AntiSec
description: As part of Operation AntiSec, the related hackergroups Anonymous and LulzSec hack several political and commercial entities and publish data, often times including confidential information. The hacked organisations include police and cyberterrorism agencies in the USA, Italy and Brazil, US government contractors and multinational businesses.

name: Anonymous vs. Zimbabwe 2013
description: Hacker collective Anonymous Africa attacks Zimbabwean Ministry of Defence, media outlets and South Africa's ANC to protest Robert Mugabe.

name: Anonymous vs. Swaziland
description: Hacker collective Anonymous Africa takes down government websites of Swaziland and Zimbabwe for alleged crimes against democracy.

name: Anonymous vs. Anti-White-Movemnets
description: Anonymous Attacks Anti-White Movements in South Africa and Zimbabwe

name: Anonymous vs. Zimbabwean Government
description: Anonymous conducted a series of cyber attacks on the Zimbabwe government websites for #ZimShutDown2016 or #ShutDownZimbabwe, a protest movement in which citizens are protesting against the Robert Mugabe’s government who has been in power for last 36 years. Sites went offline for several hours.

name: Uzbekistan attack on dissidents
description: Actors tied to the uzbek secret service used various zero-days to spy on different dissident groups in Uzbekistan, reportedly with the help of israeli based IT-company Candiru and its spyware.

name: Anonymus attack on various Venezuelean Government Pages
description: Anonymous Venezuela hacks and defaces websites of police, military and leaves anti-government remarks.

name: The Binary Guardians
description: Hackers disrupted and shut down dozens of Venezuelan government and state-backed websites, pledging online support to a protest campaign against the country's leader, Nicolas Maduro.

name: HDP vs. Hezbollah
description: A hacking group called HDP hacked the Venezuelan intelligence database to obtain personal data of alleged Hezbollah operators living under the protection of President Nicolas Maduro and leaked information from it. According to the group, this campaign was carried out together with former intelligence officers.

name: OceanLotus vs. Asean
description: OceanLotus accessed networks of local SEA governments, to use the mass taging points against the local organisation ASEAN.In the same campaign attacks were started against local humanrights defenders
initiator_name: APT32/Ocean Lotus/Sea Lotus/Canvas Cyclone fka BISMUTH
receiver_subcategory: ['Energy', 'Civil service / administration', 'Government / ministries', 'Political opposition / dissidents / expats', 'Police', 'Other social groups', 'Military']

description: Espionage-Hacks against Vietnamese Dissidents and Journalists, as well as foreign governments.

name: Criminal threat actor XE Group exploited Progress Telerik vulnerability to infiltrate a US government agency alongside other threat actors beginning in August 2021
description:
According to the US Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA), several groups, including the criminal threat actor XE Group, breached a federal civilian executive branch (FCEB) agency between November 2022 and January 2023. Attackers gained access via an unpatched Microsoft Internet Information Services (IIS) web server by exploiting a known critical vulnerability (CVE-2019-18935) in Progress Telerik's user interface (UI) software, which facilitates remote code execution.
Besides XE Group, CISA linked active exploitation to an unnamed APT. In a report from 7 December 2021, Volexi

name: Bauxit War
description: Vietnamese malware infects Vietnamese computers to disrupt and spy on their owners trying to squelch opposition to Chinese bauxite mining efforts in Vietnam, according to Human Rights Watch and McAfee.
receiver_subcategory: ['Political opposition / dissidents / expats', 'Advocacy / activists (e.g. human rights organizations)']

name: APT32/Ocean Lotus Group
description: Espionage-Hacks against Vietnamese Dissidents and Journalists, as well as foreign governments.
initiator_name: APT32/Ocean Lotus/Sea Lotus/Canvas Cyclone fka BISMUTH

description: OceanLotus accessed networks of local SEA governments, to use the mass taging points against the local organisation ASEAN.In the same campaign attacks were started against local humanrights defenders

receiver_subcategory: ['Energy', 'Civil service / administration', 'Government / ministries', 'Political opposition / dissidents / expats', 'Police', 'Other social groups', 'Military']

name: Ocean Lotus Espionage
description: The state-sponsored Vietnamese hacking group "OceanLotus" conducted an espionage campaign on the vietnamese activist Bui Thanh Hieu in Germany, the organization Vietnamese Overseas Intitative for Conscience Empowerment (VOICE) in the Phillipines and an unnamed activist in Vietnam.

name: State-sponsored Vietnamese hacking group OceanLotus gained access to a Vietnamese unspecified agribusiness
description:
State-sponsored Vietnamese hacking group OceanLotus gained access to an unspecified Vietnamese agribusiness, the US-based IT security firm Elastic Security Labs assessed with moderate confidence on 9 June 2023. 
Elastic tracks this activity as REF2754. The agricultural business concerned is identified as a critical component of both food production and distribution.
OceanLotus used the malware loaders DONUTLOADER and P8LOADER as well as POWERSEAL and the previously unknown SPECTRALVIPER malware. Elastic assesses the intrusion was conducted for intelligence gathering purposes.
initiator_name: APT32/Ocean Lotus/Sea Lotus/Canvas Cyclone fka BISMUTH

name: State-sponsored Vietnamese hacking group OceanLotus gained access to a Vietnamese financial service
description:
The state-sponsored Vietnamese hacking group OceanLotus gained access to a Vietnamese financial service, the US-based IT security firm Elastic Security Labs assessed with high confidence twice, on 7 March 2022 and in a subsequent report on 9 June 2023.
Elastic tracks the activity as REF4322. This case designation is based on the malware and technology used as well as the observed victimology. The financial service affected is identified as managing capital for business acquisitions and former state-owned enterprises.
In its first report from 2022, Elastic already attributed the cyber incident
initiator_name: APT32/Ocean Lotus/Sea Lotus/Canvas Cyclone fka BISMUTH

name: A Yemeni Houthi-aligned hacker group infected the cell phones of military personnel in various Middle Eastern countries with the GuardZoo malware beginning in October 2019
description:
A Yemeni Houthi-aligned hacker group infected the cell phones of military personnel in various Middle Eastern countries with the GuardZoo malware beginning in October 2019, the US IT security firm Lookout reported in a report on 9 July 2024.
The report stated that the hacker group enticed end users to install the GuardZoo surveillanceware, a variant of the Dendroid RAT spyware leaked in 2014, on their devices. They used military and religious themes to trick users into downloading the software through WhatsApp, WhatsApp Business, or as a downloadable file in a web browser.

description: The hackergroup Yemeni electronic army defaced the website of the indian ministry of energy efficiency
